tasks.name: Fix Lightning RMB bounce upgrade
tasks.fullName: Fix Lightning character RMB bounce improvement not working when selected
tasks.description: When playing as the Lightning character, selecting the 'bounce' improvement for the Right Mouse Button ability does not actually cause the projectile to bounce. The upgrade appears selectable but has no effect in gameplay. Investigate and fix the bounce logic for the Lightning RMB spell.
tasks.summary: The Stormcaller's Chain Lightning upgrade now works correctly. When players select the bounce improvement for their RMB ability, lightning will chain from the first enemy hit to up to 2 additional nearby enemies, dealing damage and showing beam visuals for each chain link. The upgrade stacks, allowing even more bounces.

tasks.name: Fix Lightning kill-nearby-enemies upgrade
tasks.fullName: Fix Lightning character upgrade that should kill nearby enemies not working
tasks.description: The Lightning character has an upgrade that should emit lightning to kill nearby enemies when selected, but it does not function. The visual effect and/or damage logic is missing or broken. Ensure the upgrade triggers correctly and deals damage to nearby enemies.
tasks.summary: The Stormcaller's Storm Shield upgrade now works correctly. When selected, lightning automatically strikes a random nearby enemy every second within 120px range, dealing 1 damage with a visible purple lightning bolt effect. Players will see the passive aura actively zapping enemies around them.

tasks.name: Differentiate character LMB abilities
tasks.fullName: Make each character's Left Mouse Button ability unique and distinct
tasks.description: Currently almost all characters share the same or very similar LMB attack. Each character should have a visually and mechanically distinct primary attack that reflects their element/theme (e.g. Lightning could chain, Fire could ignite, Ice could slow).
tasks.summary: Every character now has a mechanically unique primary attack (LMB). Necromancer drains life on hit, Knight's shield passes through enemies, Paladin's smite explodes on impact, Ranger's arrows pierce through targets, Druid's thorns slow and poison, Engineer's wrench tracks enemies, and Chronomancer's bolts briefly freeze targets. Each also has a distinctive visual style — spinning shields, golden radiance, thorny vines, clock-face distortions, and more.

tasks.name: Differentiate character RMB abilities
tasks.fullName: Make each character's Right Mouse Button ability unique and distinct
tasks.description: Currently almost all characters share the same or very similar RMB attack. Redesign each character's secondary ability to feel distinct and thematic.
tasks.summary: Every character now has a mechanically unique RMB ability. Cryomancer's Frost Nova became a directional Freeze Breath cone that slows enemies. Necromancer's Death Wave became a homing Death Coil that drains life. Chronomancer's Time Warp became a placed Temporal Field zone that slows and stuns. Knight's Shield Bash became Shield Rush, a short dash that stuns enemies on impact. Cone and Leap abilities now properly apply slow and stun effects.

tasks.name: Differentiate character Q abilities
tasks.fullName: Make each character's Q ability unique instead of generic damage circle
tasks.description: Most characters currently have a Q ability that is just a circle of damage. Replace these with unique abilities per character that reinforce their identity (e.g. Lightning could have a chain stun, Ice could have a freeze zone, Fire could have a lingering burn area).
tasks.summary: Each character's Q ability is now unique and thematic. Pyromancer rains 3 scattered meteors with lingering burn zones. Stormcaller's thunder chains lightning to nearby enemies with stun. Cryomancer's blizzard freezes enemies who stay inside. Necromancer's plague pulls enemies to center and drains life. Arcanist fires 5 homing arcane missiles. Paladin creates a self-centered holy zone that heals while damaging enemies. Monk emits a chi burst that knocks back enemies and heals.

tasks.name: Differentiate character R abilities
tasks.fullName: Make each character's R (ultimate) ability unique and distinct
tasks.description: Characters currently share similar R abilities. Each ultimate should be a signature moment that feels powerful and thematically appropriate for the character's element.
tasks.summary: Each character's R (ultimate) ability is now more distinct. Stormcaller fires chain lightning that arcs between enemies instead of random bolts. Ranger fires a directed arrow volley cone instead of random rain. Druid summons thorn zone rings and treant allies instead of a generic freeze-all. Monk now has knockback and invulnerability during Thousand Fists. All 6 ultimate upgrades (Quick Charge, Ult Mastery, Overflow, Ult Echo, Ult Regen, Skill Reset) are now fully functional — previously they were selectable but had no effect.

tasks.name: Slow down Ranger power scaling
tasks.fullName: Adjust Ranger power curve to delay power fantasy peak
tasks.description: The Ranger (and potentially other characters) reaches peak power fantasy too quickly, reducing the sense of progression. Adjust XP thresholds, upgrade scaling, or damage curves so power ramps more gradually and the player feels a satisfying build-up over the course of a run.
tasks.summary: Ranger power progression is now more gradual. Arrow fire rate is slower (0.25s vs 0.18s), arrows no longer pierce by default, Volley fires 4 arrows instead of 5, and Multishot adds 1 extra arrow instead of 2. Early leveling for all characters is also slightly slower with higher XP thresholds, so upgrades arrive at a more satisfying pace.

tasks.name: Add cooldown counters to skill UI
tasks.fullName: Display cooldown timer/counter on all skill icons in the HUD
tasks.description: Players currently have no visual indication of when their abilities (LMB, RMB, Q, R) will be ready to use again. Add a visible cooldown countdown or radial timer overlay on each skill icon in the HUD so players can plan their ability usage.
tasks.summary: Players now see numeric cooldown countdowns on all skill icons in the HUD. Regular abilities (LMB, RMB, Q) display seconds remaining when on cooldown, and the ultimate (R) shows its charge percentage until fully charged. This gives players clear timing information to plan their ability usage during combat.

tasks.name: Balance XP economy
tasks.fullName: Rebalance XP gain rates and level-up thresholds
tasks.description: The XP economy needs tuning. Review and adjust XP gem values, XP required per level, and XP gain rates to ensure a satisfying progression curve that doesn't peak too early or drag too long.
tasks.summary: Players now experience a smoother, more satisfying leveling curve throughout each run. Early levels come at a steady pace through the first few waves, mid-game progression ramps up gradually, and late-game levels feel earned without dragging. XP gems now accurately reflect enemy difficulty tiers, and overflow XP carries over between levels so no progress is wasted.

tasks.name: Balance health economy
tasks.fullName: Rebalance health drops, max health scaling, and damage taken
tasks.description: Health economy needs balancing. Review health pickup frequency, healing amounts, max health scaling with levels, and incoming damage to ensure players feel appropriately challenged without unfair spikes or trivial survival.
tasks.summary: Players now experience a more forgiving and consistent health economy throughout each run. Health pickups heal more in later waves (scaling from 2 to 4 HP), health drops appear more frequently as difficulty ramps up, and every boss kill guarantees recovery. Players also gain +1 max HP every 3 levels, reducing reliance on RNG upgrade rolls for survival. Assassin enemies are slightly less punishing, dealing 3 damage instead of 4.

tasks.name: Balance gold economy
tasks.fullName: Rebalance gold earn rates, drop amounts, and shop pricing
tasks.description: Gold economy needs tuning. Review gold drop rates, gold per kill, shop prices, and gold sinks to ensure meaningful economic decisions and a satisfying earn-and-spend loop.
tasks.summary: Players can now spend gold at a between-wave shop offering 5 items: Health Potion, Vitality Charm (+Max HP), Power Shard (temporary damage boost), Ward Stone (hit shield), and Swift Boots (permanent speed). Gold income has been rebalanced with wave-scaling bonuses on enemy kills (+1-3g in later waves) and a wave clear gold reward (8-65g), giving gold real mechanical meaning through meaningful earn-and-spend decisions.

tasks.name: Fix projectile wall bouncing
tasks.fullName: Fix projectiles not bouncing off walls
tasks.description: Projectiles currently pass through or stop at walls instead of bouncing off them. Investigate collision detection for projectiles against wall surfaces and implement proper reflection/bounce behavior.
tasks.summary: Projectiles now bounce off dungeon walls instead of passing through them. Player spells reflect up to 3 times by default, with the Ricochet upgrade granting additional bounces. Enemy projectiles also bounce once, making ranged enemies feel more threatening.

tasks.name: Buff Pyromancer ultimate
tasks.fullName: Rebalance Pyromancer ultimate ability (too weak)
tasks.description: The Pyromancer's ultimate ability feels underpowered compared to other characters. Review damage, area of effect, and duration, then tune upward to bring it in line with other ultimates.
tasks.summary: The Pyromancer's ultimate (Inferno) is now significantly stronger. It rains 8 meteors instead of 5, each with a larger blast radius and faster impact, bringing total base damage from 25 to 40 — matching the strongest ultimates. Each meteor also leaves a lingering burn zone on the ground for 3 seconds, adding sustained fire damage that rewards area control.

tasks.name: Buff Ranger ultimate
tasks.fullName: Rebalance Ranger ultimate ability (too weak)
tasks.description: The Ranger's ultimate ability feels underpowered compared to other characters. Review damage, utility, and impact, then tune upward to bring it in line with other ultimates.
tasks.summary: The Ranger's Arrow Rain ultimate has been significantly buffed. Players will now fire 30 arrows (up from 20) in a wider cone, with each arrow dealing 50% more damage and traveling further. Arrows also pierce through more enemies, making the ultimate feel impactful against groups — bringing it in line with other characters' ultimates.

tasks.name: Nerf overtuned ultimates
tasks.fullName: Identify and nerf overpowered ultimate abilities
tasks.description: Some characters have ultimates that are significantly stronger than others. Audit all ultimate abilities, identify outliers on the strong side, and reduce their power to bring them closer to the intended balance target.
tasks.summary: Five overpowered ultimate abilities have been reined in. Druid's Nature's Wrath spawns fewer, shorter-lived thorn zones and weaker treants. Warlock's Doom deals 35% of enemy health instead of 50%. Engineer's Mega Turret is shorter-lived with a smaller damage area. Chronomancer's Time Stop freezes enemies for less time with a smaller speed boost. Paladin's Holy Light now heals 75% instead of fully restoring both players. All other ultimates remain unchanged.

tasks.name: Research roguelike progression systems
tasks.fullName: Research how successful roguelike games structure their progression curves
tasks.description: Study progression systems in popular roguelike games (Vampire Survivors, Hades, Risk of Rain 2, etc.) to understand how they pace power growth, manage difficulty scaling, and keep runs feeling challenging throughout. Document key patterns and takeaways.
tasks.summary: Comprehensive research on progression systems from Vampire Survivors, Hades, and Risk of Rain 2 has been documented. Three highest-impact changes identified for Wizard Duel: (1) add diminishing returns via hyperbolic stacking to prevent runaway power scaling, (2) introduce time-based difficulty scaling to create urgency and prevent turtling, and (3) flatten the XP curve from exponential to stepped-linear to maintain consistent reward pacing throughout runs.

tasks.name: Audit current power scaling curve
tasks.fullName: Audit current player power scaling across a full run
tasks.description: Play through multiple runs and log how quickly the player becomes overpowered. Track XP gain rate, stat growth, ability unlock timing, and the point at which enemies stop being a threat. Identify the specific breakpoints where difficulty falls off.
tasks.summary: Comprehensive power scaling audit completed across all 20 waves, revealing that player DPS outscales enemy HP by wave 8 (the tipping point) and that bosses die in under 1 second by mid-game. The audit identifies 11 prioritized recommendations: the most critical being unbounded cross-upgrade DPS multiplication, inadequate boss HP scaling, and uncapped cooldown reduction stacking that together make late-game content trivially easy.

tasks.name: Propose progression rebalance plan
tasks.fullName: Propose a rebalance plan for progression pacing based on research
tasks.description: Using findings from the roguelike research and the power scaling audit, propose concrete changes to slow down power growth. Consider options like XP curve adjustments, enemy scaling, damage caps, diminishing returns on upgrades, or gated progression tiers.
tasks.summary: Created a comprehensive 742-line progression rebalance plan proposing 14 concrete balance changes across 3 priority tiers. The plan addresses the core issue where players become overpowered by wave 8 and bosses die in under 1 second — the proposed changes shift the power peak to waves 12-15, extend boss fights to 5-25 second encounters, and ensure late-game waves still require active play. Includes exact before/after values for every change, projected power curves, an implementation roadmap, and risk assessment.

tasks.name: Set up production build config
tasks.fullName: Configure production build pipeline and optimization settings
tasks.description: Set up production-ready build configuration with minification, tree-shaking, asset optimization, and environment-specific settings. This is the foundation for all platform-specific builds.

tasks.name: Configure desktop builds
tasks.fullName: Configure native desktop builds for macOS, Windows, and Linux
tasks.description: Set up build targets and packaging for all three desktop platforms (macOS .dmg/.app, Windows .exe/installer, Linux .AppImage/.deb). Ensure platform-specific paths, permissions, and signing requirements are handled.

tasks.name: Configure iOS build target
tasks.fullName: Configure and test iOS build and packaging
tasks.description: Set up iOS build target with proper provisioning profiles, App Store compliance, and touch input adaptation. Ensure the game runs correctly on various iPhone/iPad screen sizes.

tasks.name: Configure Android build target
tasks.fullName: Configure and test Android build and packaging
tasks.description: Set up Android build target with APK/AAB generation, Play Store compliance, and device compatibility. Handle Android-specific concerns like back button behavior and varying screen densities.

tasks.name: Configure web build and deployment
tasks.fullName: Configure web build target with hosting and deployment pipeline
tasks.description: Set up optimized web build with proper asset loading, caching strategy, and deployment pipeline. Ensure the game loads and performs well in major browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge).

tasks.name: Implement responsive UI scaling
tasks.fullName: Implement responsive UI layout for all screen sizes and aspect ratios
tasks.description: Adapt the game UI (HUD, menus, skill icons, health bars) to work across desktop monitors, mobile screens, and variable browser window sizes. Ensure readability and usability at all supported resolutions.

tasks.name: Add touch input support
tasks.fullName: Add touch controls and input adaptation for mobile platforms
tasks.description: Implement touch-based controls for spell casting, movement, and ability activation on iOS/Android. Map existing mouse/keyboard controls to intuitive touch equivalents (tap, swipe, virtual buttons).

tasks.name: Optimize performance per platform
tasks.fullName: Profile and optimize performance for mobile and low-end targets
tasks.description: Profile the game on each target platform and optimize rendering, asset sizes, and memory usage. Mobile and web targets will likely need reduced particle effects, texture compression, and frame budget tuning.

tasks.name: Set up CI/CD for all platforms
tasks.fullName: Set up CI/CD pipeline for automated multi-platform builds and testing
tasks.description: Create an automated build pipeline that produces artifacts for all target platforms (desktop, mobile, web) on each commit/release. Include automated smoke tests and build verification.

tasks.name: Add platform-specific testing matrix
tasks.fullName: Establish testing matrix and QA checklist for each target platform
tasks.description: Define a testing matrix covering all supported platforms, OS versions, and device categories. Create a QA checklist for platform-specific issues (input, rendering, audio, permissions) to validate before each release.

tasks.name: Implement hyperbolic stacking formula
tasks.fullName: Add diminishing returns to percentage-based upgrade stacking
tasks.description: Replace linear stacking for percentage-based upgrades (cooldown reduction, crit chance, etc.) with Risk of Rain 2-style hyperbolic stacking: `1 - 1/(1 + base × stacks)`. This prevents upgrades from approaching 100% and keeps late-game scaling in check. Currently all stacking is linear/unlimited.
tasks.summary: Percentage-based upgrades (crit chance, dodge, life steal, XP boost, spell mirror, raise dead) now use diminishing returns instead of stacking linearly. Players can no longer reach 100% on any probability stat no matter how many upgrades they pick, keeping late-game builds powerful but balanced.

tasks.name: Add logarithmic scaling for flat upgrades
tasks.fullName: Add diminishing returns to flat damage upgrades after 3rd stack
tasks.description: For flat upgrades like Spell Power (+1 damage per stack), introduce logarithmic scaling after the 3rd stack so early stacks feel impactful but later stacks have diminishing returns. Currently Spell Power gives +1 every time with no ceiling.
tasks.summary: Flat stackable upgrades (Spell Power, Primary Boost, Piercing, Chain Hit, Vitality, Armor, Chain Lightning) now have logarithmic diminishing returns after the 3rd stack. Players still get the full upgrade value for the first 3 picks, but subsequent picks give progressively less — preventing uncapped linear power growth while keeping early upgrade choices feel impactful.

tasks.name: Add time-based difficulty scaling
tasks.fullName: Add elapsed-time component to enemy difficulty scaling
tasks.description: Track elapsed game time and factor it into enemy HP and damage scaling alongside wave number. Even a simple multiplier like `timeMul = 1 + elapsedMinutes × 0.05` would create urgency and punish slow/turtle strategies. Currently difficulty is purely wave-based with no time pressure — players can take infinite time between waves.
tasks.summary: Enemies now scale with elapsed game time, not just wave number. Players who take too long between waves will face tougher enemies with more HP and higher damage, creating urgency and punishing turtle strategies. The scaling is gentle (+5% per minute) so it rewards efficient play without being punishing early on.

tasks.name: Flatten XP curve to stepped linear
tasks.fullName: Replace exponential XP curve with stepped linear growth
tasks.description: Replace the current ×1.25 exponential XP growth with Vampire Survivors-style stepped linear growth (e.g., +15 per level early, +20 mid, +30 late). The current curve makes level-ups too infrequent by mid-game — by level 10 the threshold is ~186 XP against enemies dropping 1-3 XP each. Goal: players should level up at least once per wave through mid-game.
tasks.summary: Level-up pacing is now smoother and more predictable. Instead of an exponential curve that made mid-game level-ups painfully slow, XP thresholds now grow by fixed amounts in three tiers: +15 per level early game, +20 mid game, +30 late game. Players should consistently level up at least once per wave through mid-game.

tasks.name: Add per-upgrade stack caps
tasks.fullName: Limit how many times a specific upgrade can be stacked
tasks.description: Introduce a maximum number of stacks per upgrade (3-5 depending on upgrade type) to create a natural power ceiling. This forces players to diversify their builds rather than stacking a single overpowered upgrade. Modeled after Vampire Survivors' slot cap system.
tasks.summary: Every stackable upgrade now has a maximum number of stacks (3-5 depending on type), creating a natural power ceiling that forces players to diversify their builds rather than repeating the same upgrade indefinitely. The upgrade selection screen shows stack counts (e.g. 2/5) and stops offering upgrades that have reached their cap.

tasks.name: Add upgrade evolution system
tasks.fullName: Evolve upgrades into qualitative variants at max stacks
tasks.description: When a player reaches the stack cap on an upgrade, offer an evolution into a qualitatively different version (similar to weapon evolution in Vampire Survivors). For example, max-stacked Spell Power could evolve into 'Spell Mastery' which changes projectile behavior rather than adding more damage. This rewards commitment to a build path.
tasks.summary: Players who commit to stacking a specific upgrade now unlock a powerful evolution once they hit the cap — 10 new evolved variants (like Spell Mastery, Void Lance, Storm Lord) appear as golden EVOLUTION cards in the upgrade selection screen. This rewards build specialization with qualitatively different power-ups rather than just more of the same stat.

tasks.name: Introduce new enemy types per wave tier
tasks.fullName: Scale enemy quality with new types and behaviors at wave thresholds
tasks.description: Add distinct enemy types and attack patterns that appear at specific wave thresholds (e.g., ranged enemies at wave 5, shielded enemies at wave 10, teleporting enemies at wave 15). Currently waves 1-5 feel the same as 15-20 except for HP/count numbers. New enemy compositions should force players to adapt strategies.
tasks.summary: Players now encounter 4 new enemy types at distinct wave thresholds that force strategic adaptation: Bombers (wave 5+) explode on death dealing area damage, Teleporters (wave 10+) blink near players to counter kiting, Splitters (wave 8+) break into two smaller enemies on death, and Berserkers (wave 12+) speed up dangerously as their health drops. Each wave tier now feels mechanically distinct rather than just scaling numbers.

tasks.name: Define fixed run length and arc
tasks.fullName: Create a defined 20-wave run arc with climactic finale
tasks.description: Design a target run structure of ~20 waves / ~15 minutes with a difficulty curve that makes reaching the end feel like an achievement. Add a climactic final encounter (e.g., an empowered final boss) rather than allowing infinite waves. This gives runs a satisfying narrative arc like Vampire Survivors' 30-minute structure.
tasks.summary: Runs now have a defined 20-wave arc with escalating difficulty that culminates in a climactic Archlord boss fight on wave 20. Players see wave progress (Wave X/20) throughout the run, and defeating the Archlord triggers a golden victory screen with run stats. Dying before wave 20 shows the wave reached out of 20.

tasks.name: Add qualitative upgrade options
tasks.fullName: Expand upgrades that change ability behavior rather than just stats
tasks.description: Create more upgrade options that change HOW abilities work (new projectile patterns, AoE-to-single-target conversion, piercing, ricochet, etc.) rather than just adding +damage/+speed. This gives players meaningful build choices in late game and prevents the upgrade pool from feeling samey. Modeled after Hades' boon system where upgrades are qualitative transformations.
tasks.summary: Players now have 8 new behavior-changing upgrades to choose from during level-ups: Boomerang (projectiles return for double hits), Volatile (projectiles explode on expiry), Fork (kills spawn extra projectiles from corpses), Gravity Pull (projectiles drag enemies toward them), Spectral (projectiles pass through walls), Frozen Touch (chance to freeze on hit), Seeker Mines (kills drop proximity explosives), and Barrage Mode (primary fires 3-shot bursts). These upgrades create meaningful build diversity by transforming how abilities work rather than just boosting stats.

tasks.name: Improve terrain visuals
tasks.fullName: Improve terrain visual quality and effects
tasks.description: Enhance terrain rendering with better textures, lighting, and environmental effects to make the battlefield more visually appealing and immersive.
tasks.summary: The dungeon battlefield now features significantly richer visuals — branching crack networks and moss patches on stone floors, an elaborate animated rune circle with hexagram, rotating glyphs and sparking energy, flickering wall torches that cast warm light pools, drifting atmospheric fog, and enhanced pillars with rune carvings and debris. The overall atmosphere is more immersive and magical while staying true to the dark dungeon aesthetic.

tasks.name: Improve monster animations
tasks.fullName: Improve monster visual design and animations
tasks.description: Add or refine monster idle, attack, hit reaction, and death animations to make encounters feel more dynamic and satisfying.
tasks.summary: Monsters now have visible hit reactions (white flash), satisfying death animations (shrink + fade + spin), and attack wind-up pulses that telegraph incoming damage. Previously static enemies like the Necromancer, Shield Bearer, and Splitter got unique idle animations, the Swarm Bat has proper wings, and the Archlord boss now has an imposing crowned silhouette with orbiting particles.

tasks.name: Improve character animations
tasks.fullName: Improve player character visuals and animations
tasks.description: Polish character models and animations including casting, movement, idle, and ability-specific effects to better convey each character's identity and actions.
tasks.summary: Player characters now feel more alive with 5 new animation effects: idle bobbing when standing still, a leaning squash-stretch bounce when moving, a radial glow burst when casting spells, a red flash when taking damage, and a smooth shrink-and-fade death animation instead of instantly disappearing. All 14 character classes benefit from these improvements.

tasks.name: Add spell visual effects polish
tasks.fullName: Polish spell and ability visual effects
tasks.description: Enhance particle effects, projectile trails, impact visuals, and screen feedback for spells to make combat feel more impactful. Covers LMB, RMB, Q, and ultimate abilities across all characters.
tasks.summary: Spell visual effects are now significantly more polished across all characters. Particles and trails glow with soft halos, explosions have element-specific effects (fire embers drift upward, ice shatters with a blue flash, lightning crackles with spark beams, dark magic implodes inward), shockwaves render with dual-ring depth, beams have a bright multi-layered glow, damage numbers are outlined for readability, and screen flashes are now color-tinted to match the element being cast.

tasks.name: Add environmental VFX
tasks.fullName: Add environmental and ambient visual effects
tasks.description: Add ambient visual effects like fog, dust particles, lighting shifts, and arena atmosphere to elevate the overall visual quality of the game world.
tasks.summary: The dungeon arena now feels significantly more atmospheric and immersive. Players will notice rolling ground fog along the floor, glowing ember sparks drifting up from wall torches, a subtle ambient light that slowly shifts between purple, amber, and blue tones, darkened corners with shadow pools, a vignette effect that draws focus to the action, and dust motes that glow warm gold when passing near torchlight.

tasks.name: Security - Validate network inputs
tasks.fullName: Security: Validate class keys, upgrade indices, and message arrays from peers
tasks.description: Network messages from peers are not validated before use. cloneClassDef() crashes on invalid class key. UPGRADE_POOL access has no bounds checking. Upgrade indices arrays are not type-checked. Fix all 3 high-severity input validation gaps in network.ts and state.ts.
tasks.notes: Quick fix - 3 high-severity findings, all small effort. See backlog: sec-001, sec-002, sec-003.
tasks.summary: Multiplayer games are now protected against malformed network messages from peers. Invalid class selections, out-of-bounds upgrade picks, and corrupted upgrade choice arrays are all safely rejected instead of crashing the game. Players will experience more stable multiplayer sessions with no visible changes to normal gameplay.

tasks.name: Error Handling - Fix silent network errors
tasks.fullName: Error Handling: Replace silent catch blocks and generic error messages in network layer
tasks.description: Network send errors are silently swallowed in empty catch blocks, causing invisible desynchs. PeerJS errors show a generic message for all failure types. Add meaningful error feedback, connection health tracking, and specific error messages.
tasks.notes: 2 high-severity findings. See backlog: eh-001, eh-002, eh-003.
tasks.summary: Network errors are no longer silently swallowed — players now see specific, actionable error messages when connections fail (e.g. 'Room not found', 'Network error — check your connection') instead of cryptic 'PeerJS error' text. Repeated send failures trigger a visible 'Connection unstable' warning so players know when their game may be desyncing.

tasks.name: Type Safety - Add network message validation
tasks.fullName: Type Safety: Add runtime type guards for incoming PeerJS messages
tasks.description: External PeerJS data is cast to NetMessage with unsafe `as` and no runtime validation. Implement isNetMessage() type guard with field validation. Also validate GamePhase enum casts and add null checks on canvas element access.
tasks.summary: Co-op multiplayer sessions are now protected against malformed or malicious network messages. All incoming PeerJS data is validated at runtime before being processed — invalid messages are silently dropped instead of potentially crashing the game or causing undefined behavior. The game canvas initialization also now fails with a clear error instead of silently breaking.

tasks.name: Test Quality - Set up testing infrastructure
tasks.fullName: Test Quality: Install vitest and write first tests for combat, network, and upgrades
tasks.description: Zero tests exist. No test framework installed. No linter configured. Install vitest, add test script to package.json, and write initial tests for the most critical modules: combat.ts damage calculations, network.ts message handling, and upgrades.ts application logic.
tasks.notes: Requires planning - large effort. See backlog: tq-001.
tasks.summary: Testing infrastructure is now in place with vitest. The project has 94 automated tests covering core game systems — combat damage calculations, upgrade pool filtering, network message handling, and utility functions. Developers can now run `npm test` to validate changes before shipping.

tasks.name: Clean Architecture - Extract magic numbers to constants
tasks.fullName: Clean Architecture: Extract 30+ scattered magic numbers into named constants
tasks.description: Fury multiplier 1.5, momentum cap 0.2, ult charge 5/15, knockForce 300, iframes values (0.2-1.5), and 20+ other magic numbers are scattered through combat.ts, physics.ts, and dungeon.ts. Extract into SPELL_BALANCE and TIMING constant objects in constants.ts.
tasks.summary: Over 120 scattered magic numbers across combat, physics, dungeon, wave, and enemy systems have been extracted into 7 named constant objects (COMBAT, TIMING, ULTIMATE, RANGES, ENEMY_AI, DUNGEON_TIMING, WAVE_PHYSICS). Game balance values like fury multiplier, ult charge rates, iframe durations, and knockback forces are now centralized in constants.ts, making future balance tuning significantly easier without hunting through implementation code.

tasks.name: Domain Logic - Fix async respawn race and damage stacking
tasks.fullName: Domain Logic: Fix racey setTimeout respawn and document damage modifier stacking
tasks.description: Respawn logic uses setTimeout which is racey - zombie respawn possible if wave changes before timeout fires. Damage modifiers stack multiplicatively in undocumented order (potential 7.2x). Move respawn to synchronous game loop tick. Document or cap damage modifier stacking.
tasks.summary: Players can no longer 'zombie respawn' after the game ends — respawning is now blocked once the game reaches Victory or Game Over. Damage from stacked buffs (fury, blood rage, crits, momentum) is now capped at 8× base damage to prevent one-shot burst kills. Enemies can no longer spawn with zero health due to edge-case scaling.

tasks.name: Fix mouse unresponsive on game over
tasks.fullName: Fix mouse cursor becoming unresponsive on game over screen after player death
tasks.description: When the player dies and the game over screen appears, the mouse cursor becomes unresponsive and the player cannot interact with UI elements including the 'Try Again' button. Likely the game state or input handling is not properly transitioning to the game over UI layer, causing pointer events to be blocked or not registered. Need to ensure mouse input is re-enabled and routed to the game over overlay so players can click 'Try Again' and other buttons.
tasks.summary: Players can now interact with the Game Over and Victory screens after dying or winning. The mouse cursor is properly restored and the 'Try Again' / 'Play Again' buttons are fully clickable, fixing the issue where the cursor was invisible and unresponsive on those screens.

tasks.name: Fix Dead Rising spell not working
tasks.fullName: Fix Dead Rising spell failing to convert enemies into necromancers
tasks.description: The Dead Rising ability is completely non-functional — no enemies are being converted into necromancers when the spell is cast. Investigate the spell logic, targeting, and conversion mechanics to ensure enemies properly transform into necromancers upon activation.
tasks.summary: The Raise Dead upgrade now works as intended — when players with this upgrade kill enemies, there's a 25% chance (per stack) the enemy is converted into a friendly minion that fights alongside the player for 5 seconds. Players will see a green 'RAISED!' text and particle effect when conversion triggers. The Lich King evolution's +50% bonus also applies correctly.

tasks.name: Fix Engineer turret visuals
tasks.fullName: Engineer turrets render as plain circles instead of turret sprites
tasks.description: Turrets summoned by the Engineer class display as simple circles rather than actual turret graphics. Replace the placeholder circle rendering with proper turret visual assets so players can clearly identify deployed turrets.
tasks.summary: Engineer turrets now display as proper rotating turrets with hexagonal bases, barrels, and muzzle flash effects instead of plain fire circles. Players can clearly see deployed turrets on the battlefield with a subtle dashed range indicator showing the turret's area of effect.

tasks.name: [Engineer] Improve v
tasks.fullName: [Engineer] Improve visual quality of RBM and R abilities/assets
tasks.description: The current visuals for RBM and R are insufficient and need polish. Review and upgrade these assets to match the expected quality bar, ensuring they are clearly readable and visually distinct in gameplay.
tasks.summary: Engineer turret abilities now have polished, distinct visuals. The regular Deploy Turret (RMB) features a radar sweep animation, orbiting spark dots, and rotating dashed range indicator. The Mega Turret ultimate (R) now properly renders with its own enhanced zone — double-ring energy fill, electrical crackle effects, a breathing power ring, energy shield around the turret, and arcing lightning between barrel tips. Both abilities are clearly readable and visually distinct from each other during gameplay.

tasks.name: Add level progress bar to UI
tasks.fullName: Display level progression bar showing XP progress toward next level
tasks.description: Add a visible progress bar (loading-bar style) that shows how far the player is toward reaching the next level. This gives players a clear sense of progression and motivates continued play by visualizing XP accumulation in real time.
tasks.summary: Players now see a purple XP progress bar in the HUD showing how close they are to the next level, with a 'Lv' label displaying their current level. The bar fills in real time as XP is collected, giving players a clear visual sense of progression alongside health, mana, and ultimate charge.

tasks.name: Fix cursor hidden in shop screen
tasks.fullName: Fix mouse cursor hidden when opening shop, preventing purchases
tasks.description: When the shop is opened, the mouse cursor is hidden (same behavior as when the game ends), making it impossible to click on items or buy anything. The shop screen should release pointer lock / show the cursor so players can interact with shop UI elements. Likely the same cursor-hide logic that runs on game end is also triggering when the shop opens.
tasks.summary: Players can now see and use the mouse cursor when the shop opens between waves, allowing them to click on items and make purchases. Previously the cursor stayed hidden when the shop appeared, making the shop completely unusable with mouse input.

tasks.name: Create power scaling audit tasks
tasks.fullName: Create development tasks from power scaling audit for 20-wave run
tasks.description: Review the comprehensive power scaling audit for 20-wave run and create actionable development tasks based on its findings and recommendations.
tasks.summary: Created 16 structured development task cards from the power scaling audit and rebalance plan, organized into 3 implementation phases. Phase 1 covers critical fixes (cooldown floors, boss HP scaling, Split Shot nerf, damage soft cap, evolution caps), Phase 2 addresses enemy count smoothing, boss XP rewards, time pressure, and HP scaling, and Phase 3 adds polish features like elite enemies and cursed upgrades. Each task includes exact acceptance criteria, file references, dependencies, and a tuning levers reference for post-implementation balance adjustments.

tasks.name: Character ability overview popup
tasks.fullName: Show character ability overview popup on selection screen
tasks.description: When picking a character, display a popup on the left side that explains each skill. Players need to understand what abilities their chosen class has before committing to it.
tasks.summary: Players now see a detailed ability overview panel on the left side of the character selection screen. When selecting any class, the panel shows the passive ability and all four spells with their key bindings, damage, mana cost, cooldown, and special effects (burn, slow, stun, etc.) as color-coded tags. This helps players understand each class's toolkit before committing to their choice.

tasks.name: Shop appears too frequently
tasks.fullName: Reduce shop popup frequency - show only on button click
tasks.description: The shop currently appears too often and disrupts gameplay. It should only appear when the player clicks a dedicated button, not automatically. Consider giving it its own standalone UI rather than a popup.
tasks.summary: The shop no longer interrupts gameplay by popping up automatically after every wave. Players now have a dedicated SHOP button in the HUD (and can press B) to open the shop on their own terms between waves. If they skip the shop, the next wave starts after a 2-second countdown — keeping the action flowing.

tasks.name: Augments appear too frequently
tasks.fullName: Reduce augment offer frequency during gameplay
tasks.description: Augments are being offered too frequently during gameplay, disrupting the flow. Reduce the frequency of augment popups to a more balanced interval.
tasks.summary: Players will now see upgrade/augment popups roughly 33% less often during gameplay. XP thresholds have been increased across all level tiers, so level-ups are more spaced out, reducing interruptions and improving gameplay flow.

tasks.name: Game ends only when host dies
tasks.fullName: Fix win condition - game should end when any player dies, not just host
tasks.description: Currently the game only ends when the host player dies. If the other player dies, the game continues, which makes the experience unfair and uninteresting. Both players' deaths should trigger the game-over condition.
tasks.summary: Fixed the win condition so that the game now ends when any player dies, not just the host. Previously, if a non-host player died they would auto-revive after 3 seconds while the game continued — now both players' deaths are treated equally and immediately trigger the game-over screen.

tasks.name: Add class synergy system
tasks.fullName: Implement class synergy system for multiplayer team compositions
tasks.description: When two players pick certain class combinations, the game should adapt and create synergies between them. Skills could mutate or gain bonus effects based on the team composition, similar to Marvel Rivals' synergy system. This encourages strategic character selection in co-op.
tasks.summary: Players now get synergy bonuses when picking complementary class pairs in co-op multiplayer. 10 unique synergies reward strategic team composition — for example, Pyromancer + Cryomancer triggers 'Elemental Clash' granting bonus spell damage to both players, while Knight + Paladin activates 'Holy Bulwark' for extra health and armor. Synergy matches are previewed during character selection and announced with an animated banner when the game starts.

tasks.name: Projectiles invisible for P2
tasks.fullName: Fix projectiles not rendering for the joining (non-host) player
tasks.description: The player who joins the game (non-host) cannot see projectiles. This is a critical multiplayer rendering/sync bug that makes the game unplayable for the second player.
tasks.summary: The second player (P2) can now see all projectiles during multiplayer games. Previously, spell rendering data like class identity, visual effects, and trail particles were not sent over the network, causing projectiles to appear invisible or as barely-visible generic orbs for the joining player. Now all spell visuals render identically for both players.

tasks.name: Enlarge skill buttons UI
tasks.fullName: Make skill buttons (Q, R, LMB, RMB) larger and reposition to corner
tasks.description: The skill buttons (Q, R, LMB, RMB, etc.) need to be made bigger for better visibility and usability. Reposition them to the top-right or top-left corner of the screen.
tasks.summary: Skill buttons (Q, R, LMB, RMB) are now twice as large (48x48px instead of 24x24px) with proportionally bigger text, making them much easier to read during gameplay. The buttons have been moved from the crowded bottom HUD bar to a dedicated floating panel in the top-right corner for better visibility without obstructing the action.

tasks.name: Optimize performance for low-end PCs
tasks.fullName: Improve game performance for weaker hardware - fix lag and low FPS
tasks.description: A player on a weaker PC experiences lag and low FPS. Investigate and optimize performance: look into rendering pipeline, particle effects, network sync overhead, and other potential bottlenecks to ensure smooth gameplay on lower-end hardware.
tasks.summary: Players on lower-end PCs will experience significantly smoother gameplay with higher FPS. Expensive visual effects (shadow blurs on all characters, particles, beams, and shockwaves) have been removed, particle counts are capped at 150 with reduced spawn rates across combat kills and zone effects, and ambient decorations (floating motes, embers, sparkles) have been cut in half. The game looks nearly identical but renders much faster.

tasks.name: Add skill detail info to char select
tasks.fullName: Display detailed skill descriptions on character selection screen
tasks.description: Each character's skills should show a clear description of what the skill does to enemies (damage, effects, debuffs, etc.) so players can make informed choices when picking a character.

tasks.name: Add skill preview animations
tasks.fullName: Add animated previews for each character skill on selection screen
tasks.description: Create and display a short animation or visual preview for each skill on the character selection screen, giving players a visual sense of how each ability looks and behaves before choosing a character.
tasks.summary: Players now see animated spell previews in the character selection detail panel. Each of the 4 spell cards displays a looping canvas animation matching the spell type — projectiles glide with trailing particles, beams crackle with lightning, zones pulse, ultimates build up and explode — all colored to match the class theme, helping players visually understand each ability before choosing a character.

tasks.name: Instead of text of skills, let there be character in idle state
tasks.fullName: Instead of text of skills, let there be character in idle state
tasks.summary: Character selection cards now show animated idle previews of each wizard class instead of text-based skill descriptions. Players can visually see how each character looks and animates before choosing, making the selection experience more intuitive and visually engaging. The detailed spell information remains accessible in the side panel when a card is selected.

tasks.name: Add multiple lives system
tasks.fullName: Implement multiple lives per match in single-player
tasks.description: Players should have several lives (respawns) per match instead of a single life. Design and implement a lives counter, respawn logic, and death/respawn flow for single-player duels.
tasks.summary: Players now have 3 lives in single-player mode. When a player dies, they respawn at the center of the arena with full health and a brief invulnerability window instead of immediately getting a game over. The HUD displays remaining lives as hearts, and the game-over screen now shows how many lives were used during the run.

tasks.name: Add multiple lives in co-op mode
tasks.fullName: Implement shared/synchronized lives system in co-op
tasks.description: Extend the multiple lives system to co-op mode so both players share the same lives mechanic. Define whether lives are shared as a pool or per-player, and sync the lives count across networked players.
tasks.summary: Co-op matches now use a shared lives pool (3 lives). When a player dies, they respawn after 3 seconds at the center of the arena with 2 seconds of invincibility, while their partner keeps fighting. The game only ends when all players are dead and no lives remain. Lives count is displayed in the top HUD bar and synced across networked players.

tasks.name: Show synergies on co-op character select
tasks.fullName: Display class synergy info on co-op character selection screen
tasks.description: The class synergy system exists but synergy information is not visible during character selection in co-op. Show applicable synergy bonuses and descriptions on the co-op character selection screen so players can make informed team composition choices.
tasks.summary: Players in co-op mode now see a full Team Synergies section on the character selection screen. For each class, all possible synergies are listed with the partner class, synergy name, flavor text, and specific mechanical bonuses each player receives. When the ally picks a matching class, that synergy entry is highlighted, helping players make informed team composition choices.

tasks.name: The guest is (not host) very laggy, we have to fix it. so the quality (performance) is the same
tasks.fullName: The guest is (not host) very laggy, we have to fix it. so the quality (performance) is the same
tasks.summary: Guest players in co-op mode now experience smooth, lag-free gameplay matching the host's quality. Entity movement is interpolated at 60fps instead of snapping every 50ms, the guest's own wizard responds instantly to input via client-side prediction, and all visual effects (hit particles, damage numbers, shockwaves) now appear on both screens. Enemy animations like hit flashes and death effects are also preserved on the guest side.

tasks.name: i think when in co-op i see the augments of my partner, not mine. i do not have blink, but it suggested me to upgrade the blink
tasks.fullName: i think when in co-op i see the augments of my partner, not mine. i do not have blink, but it suggested me to upgrade the blink
tasks.summary: Fixed a co-op bug where the guest player saw the host's augment choices during the upgrade screen instead of their own. Now each player independently generates upgrade options based on their own class and taken augments, so both players see the correct class-specific and stackable upgrades.

tasks.name: Fix guest movement lag
tasks.fullName: Fix guest player movement lag caused by performance optimization
tasks.description: The recent guest performance optimization (interpolation/client prediction) introduced movement lag for the guest player. Need to deeply investigate the root cause of the stuttering/lag between movements and find a proper solution that maintains both smooth movement and good performance.
tasks.summary: Guest player movement no longer stutters or lags during co-op play. The fix replaces the aggressive per-frame position correction (which fought against client prediction every frame) with a smart error-threshold system that only corrects when the prediction drifts significantly. Spell visuals on the guest side also no longer clip through walls between network updates.

tasks.name: Add ultimate descriptions to popups
tasks.fullName: Add unique ultimate ability descriptions to character info popups
tasks.description: Each character's info popup currently shows the same generic text 'Ultimate ability. Charges through combat.' instead of describing what the ultimate actually does. Update the popup to display a unique, informative description for each character's ultimate ability so players understand what each ultimate does before selecting a character.
tasks.summary: Each character's ultimate ability now shows a unique description on the character select screen instead of the generic 'Charges through combat' text. Players can read what each ultimate actually does — like Pyromancer raining meteors or Paladin healing all players — before choosing their character.

tasks.name: Unique ultimate animations per character
tasks.fullName: Add unique ultimate ability animations for each character
tasks.description: All characters currently share the same animation when using their ultimate ability. Create and assign distinct ultimate animations for each character to visually differentiate their ultimates and improve gameplay clarity.
tasks.summary: Each of the 14 characters now has a unique, visually distinct animation when casting their ultimate ability. Players will see fire vortexes for Pyromancer, ice crystal bursts for Cryomancer, lightning coronas for Stormcaller, and 11 other themed animations — making it immediately clear which ultimate is being used during gameplay.

tasks.name: Enforce ability cooldown floors
tasks.fullName: Add minimum cooldown floors for primary, secondary, and Q abilities
tasks.description: Add CD_FLOOR_PRIMARY (0.15s), CD_FLOOR_SECONDARY (1.0s), CD_FLOOR_Q (2.0s) constants to src/constants.ts and enforce them in src/systems/combat.ts via Math.max(cdFloor, baseCd * allMultipliers). Prevents 100-projectile-per-second scenarios from stacking Rapid Fire + Swift Cast + Spell Mastery while still allowing a meaningful 2.3x fire rate increase. Part of P1 Critical tier — must ship with other P1 changes.
tasks.summary: Ability cooldowns now have minimum floors that prevent stacking multiple cooldown reduction upgrades from reaching absurdly fast fire rates. Primary abilities can't go below 0.15s, secondary below 1.0s, and Q abilities below 2.0s — so players who stack Rapid Fire + Swift Cast + Spell Mastery still get a meaningful ~2.3x fire rate boost but can no longer reach machine-gun levels of spell spam.

tasks.name: Rework boss HP to exponential scaling
tasks.fullName: Replace linear boss HP formula with exponential scaling curve
tasks.description: Replace current boss HP formula (baseHP + wave*4/5 * timeMul) with exponential: ceil(baseHP * (1.4 ^ (wave/5)) * timeMul) in src/systems/dungeon.ts. Add BOSS_HP_EXPONENT (1.4) and BOSS_HP_EXPONENT_DIVISOR (5) to src/constants.ts. Targets: Golem w5=30HP, Demon w10=53HP, Golem w15=62HP, Archlord w20=267HP. Addresses bosses dying in under 1 second due to linear HP vs multiplicative player DPS.
tasks.summary: Boss HP now scales exponentially instead of linearly, making bosses significantly tougher in later waves. Players will notice that mid-game and late-game bosses (Golem wave 15, Demon wave 10, Archlord wave 20) no longer die in under a second — their HP grows multiplicatively to keep pace with stacking player damage upgrades.

tasks.name: Implement boss damage reduction phase
tasks.fullName: Add 50% damage reduction phase when bosses drop below 50% HP
tasks.description: For wave 15+ bosses, add a damage reduction phase that triggers at 50% HP: 50% damage reduction lasting 3 seconds. Requires new boss state tracking (phase flag, timer) in src/systems/combat.ts. Add a clear visual indicator (shield effect / glow change / grayed-out damage numbers) so players understand the mechanic. Guarantees minimum boss fight duration even against extreme builds. Part of P1 Critical tier.
tasks.summary: Wave 15+ bosses now enter a 3-second damage reduction phase when they drop below 50% HP, cutting all incoming damage in half. Players see a pulsing blue shield effect and 'HARDENED!' text when the phase triggers, plus 'RESISTED' feedback on each hit during the phase. This guarantees a minimum boss fight duration even against extreme damage builds.

tasks.name: Reduce Split Shot side bolt damage
tasks.fullName: Apply 60% damage multiplier to Split Shot side projectiles
tasks.description: Change Split Shot side bolts from 100% to 60% damage of the primary projectile in src/systems/combat.ts projectile spawn logic. Add SPLIT_SHOT_SIDE_DAMAGE_MULT (0.6) to src/constants.ts. Central bolt remains full damage for single-target boss DPS. Effective DPS multiplier drops from 2.2-3.0x to 1.6-2.2x — still one of the best upgrades but no longer a 'win the game' button that overshadows every other option.
tasks.summary: Split Shot side bolts now deal 60% damage instead of 100%, reducing the effective DPS multiplier from 2.2-3.0x to 1.6-2.2x. The central bolt remains full damage so single-target boss DPS is preserved. Split Shot is still a strong upgrade but no longer overshadows every other option.

tasks.name: Implement global bonus damage soft cap
tasks.fullName: Add hyperbolic soft cap to total combined flat bonus damage from all sources
tasks.description: Aggregate all flat damage bonuses (Spell Power, Primary Boost, evolution flat bonuses) before applying combat damage. First 6 bonus damage is uncapped; beyond 6, apply hyperbolic diminishing: effectiveBonus = 6 + (excess) * (1 - 1/(1 + excess/6)). Add BONUS_DMG_SOFT_CAP_THRESHOLD (6) and BONUS_DMG_SOFT_CAP_KNEE (6) to src/constants.ts. Implement in src/systems/combat.ts where total damage is calculated. This is the single most important change for late-game balance — reduces +15 raw bonus to ~9.7 effective.

tasks.name: Cap evolution flat bonuses with parent
tasks.fullName: Make evolution upgrades replace parent flat bonus with capped total instead of stacking
tasks.description: Change evolution bonus application in src/systems/upgrades.ts so evolutions set a capped total rather than adding on top of parent stacks. Spell Mastery: caps total spell damage bonus at +7 (was +9.63 combined). Primary Overload: caps total primary bonus at +10 (was +13.72 combined). CD reduction components unchanged. Eliminates the 'second spike' where evolutions created uncapped additional flat scaling on top of already-stacked parents.
tasks.summary: Evolution upgrades (Spell Mastery and Primary Overload) no longer spike damage when taken after maxing their parent upgrade. Instead of adding flat bonuses on top, they now cap the total bonus at +7 (spell damage) and +10 (primary damage), creating a smoother power curve. Cooldown reduction and explosion effects remain unchanged.

tasks.name: Nerf Lethal Precision crit values
tasks.fullName: Reduce Lethal Precision crit chance to 25% and multiplier to 2.5x
tasks.description: Adjust Lethal Precision evolution: crit chance from +30% to +25%, crit multiplier from 3x to 2.5x. Update LETHAL_PRECISION_CRIT_CHANCE (0.25) and LETHAL_PRECISION_CRIT_MULT (2.5) in src/constants.ts. Average damage multiplier drops from 2.22x to 1.84x (17% reduction). Makes crit builds still strong but no longer strictly dominant over all other build paths, improving build diversity.

tasks.name: Smooth enemy count curve formula
tasks.fullName: Replace 3-tier enemy count formula with single smooth curve
tasks.description: Replace current 3-tier enemy count formula (which jumps 79% at wave 8 from 19 to 34 enemies) with single smooth formula: count = floor(5 + wave*2.5 + floor(wave/8)*wave) in src/systems/dungeon.ts. Eliminates the wave 8 XP windfall that grants 2+ instant levels and creates a snowball effect. Total run XP decreases ~15% (1-2 fewer upgrades by wave 20), helping compress the power curve. Part of P2 High tier.
tasks.summary: Enemy spawning now follows a smooth curve instead of jumping 79% at wave 8. Players will experience a gradual difficulty ramp throughout the run, eliminating the wave 8 XP windfall that previously caused snowball leveling. Overall run XP is reduced ~15%, compressing the power curve for more balanced progression.

tasks.name: Boost boss wave XP and gem drops
tasks.fullName: Increase boss XP rewards and drop 3 gems instead of 1
tasks.description: Update boss XP values to 60/80/100/150 for wave 5/10/15/20 bosses in src/systems/dungeon.ts. Change bosses to drop 3 gems instead of 1 (same total XP, more satisfying pickup). Increase boss minion count by +3 at each boss wave. Fixes boss waves giving dramatically less XP than normal waves (wave 10 boss yields ~90 XP vs ~264-336 for adjacent waves), making bosses feel rewarding instead of punishing.
tasks.summary: Boss waves now feel rewarding instead of punishing. Bosses at waves 5/10/15/20 drop significantly more XP (60/80/100/150, up from ~40/50/100), split across 3 satisfying gem pickups instead of a single drop. Each boss wave also spawns 3 additional minions, making boss encounters more epic and rewarding.

tasks.name: Add boss minion trickle-spawn timer
tasks.fullName: Spawn boss wave minions gradually over 10 seconds instead of all at once
tasks.description: Implement a timer system in src/systems/dungeon.ts that spawns boss minions over 10 seconds instead of simultaneously. Extends the encounter feel and prevents instant XP dumps. Works with the increased minion count (+3 per boss wave) to create sustained combat around the boss rather than a burst-and-done pattern. Part of P2 High tier.
tasks.summary: Boss wave minions now spawn gradually over 10 seconds instead of appearing all at once. This creates sustained combat encounters around bosses — players face a steady stream of reinforcements rather than an instant burst, making boss fights feel more dynamic and preventing instant XP dumps.

tasks.name: Increase time-based scaling factor
tasks.fullName: Raise timeMul coefficient from 0.05 to 0.12 per minute
tasks.description: Change TIME_SCALING_FACTOR from 0.05 to 0.12 in src/constants.ts. At 15 minutes, enemies will have +30% HP/damage instead of +12.5%. Creates real time pressure in late-game decision-making — slow players face meaningfully tougher enemies while fast/skilled players are rewarded. Aligns with Risk of Rain 2 model where 'time is the real enemy'. Single constant change, trivial effort.
tasks.summary: Late-game enemies now scale significantly harder over time — at 15 minutes, enemies have +30% HP and damage instead of the previous +12.5%. This creates real time pressure where slow players face meaningfully tougher fights, rewarding skilled/fast play.

tasks.name: Cap Bloodlust with crit overflow
tasks.fullName: Cap Bloodlust attack speed at +100% and add crit chance overflow
tasks.description: Cap Bloodlust (Berserker class) at +100% attack speed (20 kills). After the speed cap, each subsequent kill grants +1% crit chance, capped at +15%. Add BLOODLUST_SPEED_CAP (1.0) and BLOODLUST_CRIT_CAP (0.15) to src/constants.ts. Implement in src/systems/upgrades.ts or combat.ts. Fixes Bloodlust granting +1500% attack speed by wave 15 (16x fire rate), making Berserker strongest class by massive margin and bypassing CD floors.
tasks.summary: Berserker's Bloodlust upgrade now properly caps attack speed at +100% (20 kills) instead of scaling infinitely. After hitting the speed cap, each additional kill grants +1% crit chance up to +15%, giving Berserkers a meaningful late-game power curve without the game-breaking fire rates.

tasks.name: Add multiplicative enemy HP wave scaling
tasks.fullName: Add per-wave multiplicative component to enemy HP formula
tasks.description: Add multiplicative term to enemy HP: hp = ceil((baseHP + hpScale - 1) * timeMul * (1 + wave * 0.04)) in src/systems/dungeon.ts. Add ENEMY_HP_WAVE_MULT (0.04) to src/constants.ts. Wave 15 enemies get 1.6x HP, wave 20 gets 1.8x HP. Combined with player DPS nerfs, enemies survive 2-3 hits in late waves instead of being one-shot. Key change that makes late-game combat feel like actual combat.
tasks.summary: Late-game enemies now scale multiplicatively with wave number — at wave 15 enemies have 1.6x HP, at wave 20 they have 1.8x HP. Combined with existing difficulty scaling, this means enemies survive multiple hits in later waves instead of being one-shot, making late-game combat feel like actual combat.

tasks.name: Smooth hpScale to single formula
tasks.fullName: Replace 2-tier hpScale formula with smooth single formula
tasks.description: Replace current hpScale (1+floor(wave/4) for waves 1-10, 2+floor(wave/3) for waves 11+) with single formula: hpScale = 1 + floor(wave * 0.6) in src/systems/dungeon.ts. Eliminates the 67% HP jump at wave 11 (from 3 to 5). Proposed values are generally higher than current (wave 15: 10 vs 7, wave 20: 13 vs 8), providing a built-in HP buff alongside the multiplicative component. Trivial effort.
tasks.summary: Enemy HP scaling now increases smoothly across all waves instead of having a jarring 67% spike at wave 11. Players will experience a more gradual and predictable difficulty curve, with enemies gaining HP steadily as waves progress.

tasks.name: Implement elite enemy system
tasks.fullName: Add elite enemy variants with 2.5x HP, 1.3x damage, and 2x XP from wave 13+
tasks.description: From wave 13+: 10% of spawned enemies are elite variants. From wave 17+: 20% are elite. Elites get 2.5x HP, 1.3x damage, 2x XP on death, same speed/behavior. Add elite flag to enemy spawn system in src/systems/dungeon.ts, apply multipliers in src/systems/combat.ts. Creates mini-challenges within waves demanding target prioritization. Add elite threshold and multiplier constants to src/constants.ts. Part of P3 Polish tier.
tasks.summary: Elite enemies now appear in late-game waves, creating tougher mini-challenges. From wave 13+, 10% of spawned enemies are elite variants with 2.5x HP, 1.3x damage, and 2x XP rewards. From wave 17+, the elite rate increases to 20%. Bosses are never elite — this adds target prioritization decisions for players in late-game combat.

tasks.name: Add elite enemy golden glow visual
tasks.fullName: Implement golden glow visual effect for elite enemy variants
tasks.description: Add a golden glow shader/tint to elite enemies so they are immediately recognizable during combat. The visual creates instant hierarchy — players can spot and prioritize elites at a glance. Implement in the rendering system. Should be visually distinct but not obscure the enemy's base type/animations. Part of the elite enemy system (P3 Polish tier).
tasks.summary: Elite enemies now display a subtle pulsing golden glow behind them, making them instantly recognizable during combat. The glow animates smoothly and doesn't obscure the enemy's base appearance, so players can still identify the enemy type while quickly spotting which ones are elite-tier threats worth prioritizing.

tasks.name: Scale upgrade choices by wave tier
tasks.fullName: Increase upgrade choices from 3 to 4-5 based on wave progression
tasks.description: Change upgrade offer logic in src/systems/upgrades.ts: waves 1-11 offer 3 choices (unchanged), waves 12-15 offer 4 choices (more variety), waves 16+ offer 5 choices (one of which is cursed). Add choice count constants per wave tier to src/constants.ts. The 4-choice tier is trivial; the 5-choice tier depends on the cursed upgrade system. Fixes late-game upgrade screens becoming 'pick the least bad option' with 15+ upgrades already taken.
tasks.summary: Players now see more upgrade choices as they progress through waves. Waves 1-11 still offer 3 choices, waves 12-15 offer 4 choices for more variety, and waves 16+ offer 5 choices (including one cursed upgrade). This makes late-game upgrade screens feel less constrained when players have already taken many upgrades.

tasks.name: Design and implement cursed upgrades
tasks.fullName: Create cursed upgrade pool with benefit+drawback mechanics for waves 16+
tasks.description: Design and implement a pool of cursed upgrades that appear as the 5th choice from wave 16+. Examples: Glass Cannon (+4 all spell damage, -2 max HP), Reckless Haste (-40% all cooldowns, +50% damage taken), Blood Pact (+20% life steal, -3 max HP), Unstable Power (+8 primary damage, 5% self-damage chance). Requires new data definitions, drawback application logic, and UI indicators (red border, warning icon). Benefits should clearly outweigh drawbacks for the right build to achieve >15% pick rate.
tasks.summary: Players now see a 4th upgrade card with a dark red cursed theme starting from wave 16. Five cursed upgrades offer powerful benefits with meaningful trade-offs — like massive cooldown reduction at the cost of taking more damage, or huge primary damage with a chance to hurt yourself. Each cursed card is visually distinct with a skull icon and red styling so players can immediately recognize the risk-reward choice.

tasks.name: Playtest P1 core rebalance changes
tasks.fullName: Run structured playtests to validate P1 balance targets
tasks.description: After implementing all P1 changes (cooldown floors, boss HP, Split Shot, soft cap, evolution caps): run 3+ full 20-wave playthroughs — damage-focused Pyromancer, balanced Pyromancer, and class-specific (Berserker/Monk). Verify boss kill times (wave 10: 5-8s, wave 20: 15-25s), DPS/HP ratios stay in 2.7-4.3 range, no upgrade picked <10% or >90%. Tune BONUS_DMG_SOFT_CAP_THRESHOLD (6→8 if too hard) and CD_FLOOR_PRIMARY (0.15→0.12 if sluggish) as needed.
tasks.summary: P1 balance validation is complete. The balance simulator now supports realistic playtest runs with upgrade selection between waves, boss kill timing, DPS/HP ratio tracking, and upgrade pick rate analysis. All P1 targets validated: wave 10 boss kills in 5.5-7.2s (target 5-8s), wave 20 boss kills in 17-23s (target 15-25s), DPS/HP ratios at 2.85-2.96 (target 2.7-4.3), and no upgrade pick rate outliers. Balance constants were tuned — primary cooldown floor lowered for snappier combat, damage soft cap raised for better upgrade scaling, and the Archlord finale boss rebalanced as a proper endgame challenge.

tasks.name: Playtest P2 scaling changes
tasks.fullName: Run structured playtests to validate P2 scaling targets
tasks.description: After implementing P2 changes (enemy count, boss XP, timeMul, Bloodlust cap, HP scaling): verify XP pacing across full run (target ~20-22 upgrades by wave 20), confirm time pressure feels meaningful but not punishing, test Bloodlust cap specifically with Berserker class. Tune ENEMY_HP_WAVE_MULT (0.04→0.03 if too tanky), boss XP values if leveling feels off. Must be done after P1 is stable.
tasks.summary: P2 scaling changes have been validated through the balance simulator. The balance sim now fully simulates the Berserker's Bloodlust mechanic (attack speed cap at +100%, crit overflow to +15%) and includes P2-specific validation targets for upgrade pacing and combat balance. Core combat metrics pass — boss kill time at wave 10 is 6.7s (target 5-8s), DPS/HP ratio is 3.18 (target 2.7-4.3), and Bloodlust cap saturates correctly at ~35 kills. Boss XP values were tuned (+25 XP across mid-game bosses) to improve upgrade pacing toward the 20-22 target by wave 20.

tasks.name: Add co-op enemy HP multiplier
tasks.fullName: Apply 1.5-1.8x enemy HP multiplier for 2-player co-op mode
tasks.description: Two players means 2x total DPS with shared enemy pools, making the single-player rebalance insufficient for co-op. Add a co-op HP multiplier (1.5-1.8x) applied to all enemies when 2 players are present. Should be addressed as a separate tuning pass after single-player balance is validated. Prevents co-op from trivializing the rebalanced progression.
tasks.summary: Co-op enemies now have 1.5x more HP when two players are present. This applies to all enemies including bosses, preventing two-player co-op from trivializing the rebalanced single-player progression. Solo play is unaffected.

tasks.name: Audit current network bottlenecks
tasks.fullName: Audit and document current PeerJS network architecture bottlenecks
tasks.description: Profile and document the specific bottlenecks in the current PeerJS-based network architecture that cause guest performance degradation. Identify whether the issue is latency, message throughput, serialization overhead, connection reliability, or a combination. This analysis will inform the decision on whether to migrate to a different stack.
tasks.summary: Comprehensive network architecture audit documenting 10 bottlenecks in the PeerJS multiplayer system that cause guest performance degradation. The audit identifies unbounded state serialization, unthrottled input sending, and O(n²) enemy matching as the most critical issues, and provides 14 prioritized recommendations across 4 tiers ranked by impact-to-effort ratio. Key finding: bottlenecks are application-layer, not transport-layer — fixing serialization and send rates will improve guest experience more than migrating away from PeerJS.

tasks.name: Add delta state serialization
tasks.fullName: Replace unbounded full-state serialization with delta compression
tasks.description: network.ts:257-301 sends ALL entities every 50ms with no culling or delta. Implement delta state serialization that only sends changed entity properties since last acknowledged state, drastically reducing message size.
tasks.summary: Multiplayer state sync now uses delta compression — the host only sends data that actually changed since the last frame instead of the entire game state every 50ms. This drastically reduces network bandwidth during gameplay, especially during idle periods and between waves. A full state sync is still sent every second as a safety net to prevent desync.

tasks.name: Add input change detection
tasks.fullName: Throttle input sending with change detection instead of 60Hz spam
tasks.description: main.ts:240 sends input every frame (60Hz) with no change detection. Add input deduplication so inputs are only sent when they actually change, reducing network traffic by ~90% during idle periods.
tasks.summary: Guest players now only send input to the host when something actually changes (movement, aiming, or button presses), instead of spamming 60 messages per second. A heartbeat still fires 5 times per second to keep the connection alive. This reduces network traffic by ~90% during idle periods with no visible gameplay impact.

tasks.name: Align sim and net tick rates
tasks.fullName: Fix mismatched simulation and network update rates
tasks.description: Host runs simulation at 60 FPS but guests only receive updates at 20Hz (50ms interval), creating a 33ms perception gap. Align or interpolate between these rates to smooth guest experience.
tasks.summary: Guest players now experience smoother motion during multiplayer sessions. The game explicitly defines simulation (60 FPS) and network (20 Hz) tick rates, and the guest's interpolation speed automatically adapts to actual network packet timing instead of assuming a fixed 50ms interval — eliminating stuttery movement caused by network jitter.

tasks.name: Add client-side prediction
tasks.fullName: Implement client-side prediction for guest player movement
tasks.description: Guest player currently snaps on corrections > 50px because there is no client-side prediction. Implement prediction for guest player movement with smooth reconciliation on server corrections.
tasks.summary: Guest players no longer experience jarring teleportation snaps during network lag. Player movement now uses smooth, adaptive correction that gracefully converges to the server position regardless of error size. The other player's character also moves more fluidly between network updates using velocity-based extrapolation instead of stopping at the last known position.

tasks.name: Batch network messages
tasks.fullName: Implement message batching to reduce per-message overhead
tasks.description: Each network message is sent individually with its own framing overhead. Batch multiple messages into single packets per tick to reduce overhead and improve throughput.
tasks.summary: Co-op multiplayer network messages are now batched into single packets per frame, reducing WebRTC framing overhead. When multiple messages happen in the same frame (e.g. game state update + upgrade notification), they're combined into one transmission instead of being sent separately. Players won't notice any visible change, but the connection will be more efficient and use less bandwidth.

tasks.name: Add entity culling/LOD
tasks.fullName: Implement entity relevance culling and level-of-detail for network sync
tasks.description: All entities are sent regardless of relevance to the guest player. Add distance-based culling and level-of-detail so offscreen or distant entities send less data or are omitted entirely.
tasks.summary: Multiplayer games now use distance-based entity culling and level-of-detail to reduce network bandwidth. Enemies, spells, projectiles, and effect zones far from the guest player are no longer sent, and distant enemies send only essential position data rather than full stats. Players should see smoother performance in late-game waves with many entities on screen.

tasks.name: Reduce float precision in messages
tasks.fullName: Compress position data by reducing float precision in network messages
tasks.description: Positions are sent as full 64-bit floats. Round or quantize positions to integers or fixed-point values (e.g. Math.round) to reduce message payload size significantly.
tasks.summary: Network messages now send all numeric values with reduced precision — radii as integers, timers/ages rounded to 1 decimal, and guest input coordinates truncated. This reduces multiplayer bandwidth usage with no visible impact on gameplay.

tasks.name: Add adaptive send rate
tasks.fullName: Implement adaptive network throttling based on connection quality
tasks.description: Network send interval is fixed at 50ms regardless of connection conditions. Add adaptive throttling that increases interval during congestion and decreases during good connectivity.
tasks.summary: Multiplayer network updates now automatically adapt to connection quality. During stable connections, state syncs at the standard 50ms rate. When the connection becomes unstable (send failures), the system backs off to up to 150ms between updates to reduce congestion, then gradually recovers as conditions improve. Players will experience smoother gameplay on spotty connections instead of error messages.

tasks.name: Fix O(n²) enemy matching
tasks.fullName: Optimize quadratic enemy matching algorithm on state receipt
tasks.description: state.ts:365-414 uses O(n²) matching when reconciling enemy state. Replace with an ID-keyed Map lookup for O(n) matching, which becomes critical as enemy count grows in later waves.
tasks.summary: Multiplayer enemy synchronization now uses stable IDs instead of proximity-based guessing, eliminating performance degradation in later waves with many enemies on screen. Players will notice smoother enemy animations and fewer visual glitches during intense combat.

tasks.name: Write network audit document
tasks.fullName: Create comprehensive network architecture audit document at docs/network-audit.md
tasks.description: Document the full audit findings: architecture overview (host-authoritative, PeerJS, WebRTC data channels), all 10 identified bottlenecks with severity/location/impact, message size profiles, and the 14 prioritized recommendations across 4 tiers ranked by impact-to-effort ratio. Key conclusion: bottlenecks are application-layer, not transport-layer.
tasks.summary: A comprehensive network architecture audit document has been added at docs/network-audit.md. It covers the full multiplayer networking layer — architecture overview, message protocol analysis, bandwidth profiles by game phase, 10 identified bottlenecks with severity ratings, and 14 prioritized optimization recommendations across 4 tiers. The key finding: all bottlenecks are application-layer, meaning significant improvements are achievable without changing the WebRTC/PeerJS transport.

tasks.name: Profile & benchmark current game loop
tasks.fullName: Profile and benchmark current game loop to identify performance bottlenecks
tasks.description: Run performance profiling (Chrome DevTools, performance.now markers) on the game loop, enemy updates, spell updates, rendering, and GC pauses. Document baseline FPS, frame budget breakdown, and the top hotspots. This data will guide which systems to refactor first for maximum impact.
tasks.summary: Added a built-in performance profiler and debug overlay that players/developers can toggle with F3. The overlay shows real-time FPS, per-system frame time breakdown, entity counts, and GC pause warnings — providing the baseline data needed to identify and prioritize performance optimizations. A performance baseline document was also created cataloging the game loop architecture and 5 known hotspots.

tasks.name: Design ECS-inspired architecture
tasks.fullName: Design an ECS-inspired component/system architecture for the game
tasks.description: Design a lightweight ECS-inspired architecture suited to this Canvas2D game.
