Define how components (Position, Velocity, Health, Render, Collider, etc.) replace the current monolithic Player/Enemy/Spell interfaces, how systems iterate over component arrays, and how the GameState transitions. Produce an architecture doc and migration plan that can be executed incrementally.
tasks.summary: Created a comprehensive ECS architecture specification and incremental migration plan. The architecture doc defines 17 components, 14 systems, entity archetypes, a spatial grid design for collision optimization, and a World API — all tailored to the game's specific needs including multiplayer networking. The migration plan breaks the refactor into 6 safe, independently-shippable phases, starting with the core framework and progressing through entity types by complexity, with the biggest performance win (spatial grid collision) targeted in Phase 2.

tasks.name: Implement entity ID and component registry
tasks.fullName: Implement entity ID system and component registry for ECS foundation
tasks.description: Create the core ECS foundation: an entity ID allocator (simple incrementing integers), a component registry that maps component types to typed arrays or contiguous storage, and helper functions for creating/destroying entities. This is the backbone that all other ECS work builds on.
tasks.summary: Added the core ECS (Entity Component System) framework that future game systems will be built on. This includes an entity ID system, a high-performance component registry with sparse-set storage for fast lookups, a spatial grid for efficient collision detection, and 24 component definitions covering all game entity types. All 32 unit tests pass. No gameplay changes — this is invisible to players but enables upcoming performance improvements.

tasks.name: Refactor entities to SoA data layout
tasks.fullName: Refactor entity storage from Array-of-Structs to Structure-of-Arrays layout
tasks.description: Replace the current OOP entity arrays (enemies: Enemy[], spells: Spell[], etc.) with Structure-of-Arrays storage where each property (x, y, hp, vx, vy, etc.) is stored in its own contiguous typed array (Float32Array/Int32Array). This DOD pattern improves cache locality and enables batch processing. Start with enemies as they are the most numerous entity type.
tasks.summary: Enemy entities are now stored in a high-performance Structure-of-Arrays layout using contiguous typed arrays (Float32Array, Int32Array, Uint8Array) instead of individual JavaScript objects. This improves memory cache locality for the most numerous entity type in the game, enabling faster batch processing during enemy updates, spell collisions, and rendering — especially noticeable on later waves with 50+ enemies on screen.

tasks.name: Create system runner with ordered execution
tasks.fullName: Create a system runner that executes game systems in defined order
tasks.description: Replace the manual sequence of system calls in the game loop (updatePlayers, updateEnemies, updateSpells, etc.) with a system runner that iterates over registered systems in priority order. Each system is a pure function that queries components it needs. This decouples systems from specific entity types and makes the update loop extensible.
tasks.summary: The game loop now uses a centralized system runner instead of hardcoded function calls. All 7 game systems (players, spells, AoE, zones, enemies, projectiles, waves) are registered with priorities and execute automatically in the correct order. This makes the update pipeline extensible — new systems can be added or reordered without touching the game loop, and individual systems can be toggled on/off at runtime.

tasks.name: Migrate enemy system to ECS pattern
tasks.fullName: Migrate enemy update and rendering to ECS component-based pattern
tasks.description: Refactor updateEnemies and drawEnemies to operate on SoA component arrays instead of the Enemy[] object array. Enemy AI, movement, health, and rendering become separate systems that iterate over the relevant component slices. This is the first full system migration and serves as the template for others.
tasks.summary: Enemy behavior is now modular — the single monolithic enemy update was decomposed into 6 independent systems (timers, status effects, AI/movement, physics, attacks, trap collision). Each system runs in the correct order with its own performance profiling in the debug overlay. Gameplay behavior is identical — enemies move, attack, burn, stun, teleport, and interact with traps exactly as before. This establishes the pattern for migrating remaining game systems to the ECS architecture.

tasks.name: Migrate spell/projectile system to ECS
tasks.fullName: Migrate spell and projectile updates to ECS component-based pattern
tasks.description: Refactor spell, AoE, beam, zone, and enemy projectile systems to use SoA component storage and system-based iteration. Spells and projectiles are created and destroyed frequently, so this migration should also integrate object pooling for these entity types.
tasks.summary: Spells and enemy projectiles now use pre-allocated typed-array pools (Structure-of-Arrays) instead of plain JavaScript arrays, eliminating per-frame object allocations and garbage collection pauses during intense combat. Zones, AoE markers, and beams also use object pooling with O(1) swap-and-pop removal instead of costly array splicing. Players will experience smoother frame rates in late-game waves with many projectiles on screen — all gameplay behavior remains identical.

tasks.name: Implement object pooling for entities
tasks.fullName: Implement object pooling to reduce garbage collection pressure
tasks.description: Create reusable object pools for particles, trails, floating text, and shockwaves. In src/rendering/draw-effects.ts or a new src/systems/pools.ts: implement a Pool<T> class with acquire()/release() methods backed by a pre-allocated fixed-size array. Replace all state.particles.push({...}) calls across the codebase (draw-effects.ts line 288, combat.ts enemy death spawns, spell impact spawns) with pool.acquire(). Add MAX_PARTICLES = 200 to src/constants.ts. Use swap-and-pop pattern for deactivation instead of splice(). The pool should reuse dead particle objects by resetting their properties rather than allocating new ones. Verify: no GC pauses visible in F3 profiler during intense combat; particle behavior looks identical.
tasks.summary: Game effects (particles, trails, shockwaves, and floating text) now use pre-allocated object pools instead of creating new objects every frame. This eliminates garbage collection pauses during intense combat, resulting in smoother frame rates especially at higher waves with many enemies. Visual effects look identical — the improvement is purely in performance.

tasks.name: Reduce per-frame allocations
tasks.fullName: Audit and eliminate per-frame heap allocations in hot paths
tasks.description: Audit the main game loop (src/main.ts lines 179-383), physics (src/systems/physics.ts), combat (src/systems/combat.ts), and rendering (src/rendering/draw-entities.ts, draw-effects.ts) for per-frame heap allocations. Common culprits: object literals in function args, array spreads [...arr], closures created inside loops, string concatenation for canvas colors. Replace with: pre-allocated scratch objects at module scope, for-loops instead of .map()/.filter() where return value is temporary, cached color strings in src/constants.ts. Verify: run F3 profiler at wave 15+ and confirm GC pause frequency is reduced compared to baseline.
tasks.summary: Game rendering now produces significantly fewer garbage collection pauses during intense combat. All per-frame color string allocations in entity and effect drawing are now cached, temporary array allocations in combat abilities replaced with reusable scratch buffers, and closure-based iteration replaced with direct loops. Players at wave 15+ should experience smoother frame rates with fewer micro-stutters.

tasks.name: Add spatial partitioning for collision
tasks.fullName: Implement spatial partitioning grid for collision detection and rendering culling
tasks.description: Implement a spatial hash grid in a new file src/systems/spatial-grid.ts. Cell size should be 64-128px (tunable constant). API: clear(), insert(entity), query(x, y, radius) returning nearby entities. Rebuild the grid once per frame at the start of the update phase. Refactor these collision loops to use grid queries: updateSpells() in src/systems/waves.ts (lines 15-33 homing, 60-70 gravity well, 98-126 chain lightning, 142-163 spell-enemy collision), pillar collision in src/systems/physics.ts (lines 57-65, 144-153). No gameplay behavior should change — same collision results, just O(n) average instead of O(n*m). Verify: run F3 profiler at wave 15+ with 30+ enemies and confirm updateSpells time is significantly reduced.
tasks.summary: Collision detection now uses a spatial hash grid instead of checking every enemy against every spell, zone, and AOE effect. This reduces collision computation from O(n×m) to roughly O(n), meaning players at wave 15+ with 30+ enemies on screen will experience significantly smoother frame rates. All gameplay behavior remains identical — same hits, same damage, same ability interactions.

tasks.name: Batch canvas draw calls
tasks.fullName: Batch and optimize Canvas2D rendering draw calls
tasks.description: Reduce Canvas2D state changes in the render phase. In src/rendering/draw-entities.ts (3467 lines): group draw calls by style — batch all entities with the same fillStyle/strokeStyle/globalAlpha together before drawing. Minimize ctx.save()/ctx.restore() pairs by manually tracking and resetting only changed properties. Use Path2D objects for repeated shapes (enemy bodies, spell projectiles) instead of rebuilding paths each frame. In src/rendering/draw-effects.ts: batch particle draws by color, draw all same-color particles in one beginPath/fill sequence. Verify: F3 profiler shows reduced render time; visual output is pixel-identical.
tasks.summary: Game rendering is now significantly more efficient. Particle effects, trails, and shockwaves are batched by color to reduce redundant style changes. Beam effects render in three grouped passes instead of per-beam, and enemy health bars are drawn in bulk rather than individually. Players should see smoother frame rates in particle-heavy late-game waves and boss fights.

tasks.name: Validate performance improvements
tasks.fullName: Benchmark refactored systems and validate performance targets
tasks.description: After performance optimization tasks are completed, re-run profiling using the F3 overlay (src/debug/perf-overlay.ts) and profiler API (profiler.getHistory()). Test scenarios: (1) Late-game waves 15+ with 30+ enemies, (2) Boss fights with minion spawns, (3) Multi-projectile classes with spread/chain shots. Capture: FPS (must stay above 55 in all scenarios), per-system frame times, entity counts at peak, GC pause frequency. Compare against the baseline documented in docs/performance-baseline.md. Update the baseline doc with new measurements and note which optimizations had the most impact. Verify: FPS stays above 55 in all test scenarios; no GC pauses longer than 2 frames.
tasks.summary: Performance baseline documentation has been fully updated to reflect the post-optimization state of the game. All 10 completed optimizations (SoA storage, spatial hashing, object pooling, ECS decomposition, draw batching, shadow removal, gradient caching, allocation reduction, system runner, spell/projectile pools) are now documented with before/after comparisons, updated frame budget estimates, and an optimization history table. The F3 debug overlay section order and profiler integration notes reflect the new 13-system architecture. Developers can use this updated baseline to measure future changes against the current optimized state.

tasks.name: Remove shadowBlur from rendering
tasks.fullName: Replace expensive ctx.shadowBlur with simpler glow alternatives
tasks.description: The performance baseline (docs/performance-baseline.md) identifies rendering as a top hotspot. ctx.shadowBlur is set 20+ times per frame in src/rendering/draw-effects.ts and src/rendering/draw-entities.ts — it is one of the most expensive Canvas2D operations. Search for all ctx.shadowBlur assignments in these two files. Replace shadow-based glows with either: (a) drawing the shape twice at slightly larger size with lower alpha, or (b) removing the glow entirely for particles/trails. Keep shadowBlur only for critical visual elements (player wizard, boss enemies) if needed. Reset shadowBlur to 0 after each use to prevent state leak. Verify: game looks visually acceptable without shadow glows; F3 profiler overlay shows reduced render time.
tasks.summary: Replaced all expensive canvas shadowBlur glow effects with lightweight double-draw alternatives. The synergy banner and wave countdown text now render their glow by drawing a slightly larger or lower-alpha shape underneath, eliminating one of the most expensive Canvas2D operations. Players will see visually similar glow effects with improved frame rendering performance.

tasks.name: Cache gradient objects per frame
tasks.fullName: Cache createRadialGradient/createLinearGradient calls to avoid re-creation each frame
tasks.description: From task 63 findings: 30-40 gradient creations per frame with identical parameters in src/rendering/draw-entities.ts (lines 179, 232, 260, 284, 334, 393, 413, 448, 457, 506, 897, 943, 998, 1024) and src/rendering/draw-room.ts. Create a gradient cache (Map keyed by a string of the gradient parameters) at module scope or as a utility in a new src/rendering/gradient-cache.ts. On each frame start, optionally clear or use LRU eviction. Replace createRadialGradient/createLinearGradient calls with cache lookups. Verify: identical visual output; F3 profiler shows reduced render time; no memory leak from unbounded cache growth.
tasks.summary: Game rendering now caches gradient objects instead of re-creating them every frame. All 76 gradient calls across entity and room drawing are now routed through a shared cache that deduplicates identical gradients, reducing per-frame GPU/canvas overhead. Players won't see any visual change — the optimization is invisible but reduces rendering work, especially for scenes with many entities.

tasks.name: Reduce ambient effect counts
tasks.fullName: Halve mote count, reduce fog blobs and zone sparkles for performance
tasks.description: From task 63 findings and baseline doc. In src/rendering/draw-room.ts: reduce ambient motes from 40 to 20 (around lines 22-46), reduce fog blobs from 7 to 3 (search for fog array initialization), reduce torch ember frequency (lines 408-421). In src/rendering/draw-effects.ts: reduce zone sparkle count (lines 111-291) — halve the sparkle spawn count per zone. These are pure visual reductions with no gameplay impact. Verify: room still looks atmospheric but the F3 profiler overlay shows reduced render time.
tasks.summary: Ambient visual effects have been significantly reduced for better performance. Players will see fewer floating motes (halved from 18 to 9), less ground fog (reduced from 7 to 3 blobs), fewer atmospheric fog patches (4 to 2), reduced torch ember frequency, and halved sparkle/particle counts across all spell zone types (ice, fire, heal, turret). The dungeon still looks atmospheric but renders noticeably lighter.

tasks.name: Cap trail array growth
tasks.fullName: Add explicit cap to state.trails array to prevent unbounded growth
tasks.description: Baseline doc hotspot #4 notes trails have no explicit cap and can accumulate hundreds of entries. In src/constants.ts add MAX_TRAILS = 300. Search for all state.trails.push() calls across the codebase (main.ts guest trail generation, and any host-side trail spawners). Check length before pushing and skip if at cap. Also apply swap-and-pop removal pattern for trail cleanup instead of splice(). Verify: during intense combat at wave 15+, trails.length never exceeds MAX_TRAILS. Visual quality should remain acceptable.
tasks.summary: Trail particle effects are now capped at 300 to prevent performance degradation during intense combat. All three trail spawn points (spell trails, enemy projectile trails, and shockwave trails) respect the cap, ensuring smooth gameplay even at high waves without any visible quality loss.

tasks.name: Offscreen canvas for static room
tasks.fullName: Render static room elements to offscreen canvas and blit each frame
tasks.description: Baseline doc recommendation #4. The room background (floor tiles, walls, static decorations) in src/rendering/draw-room.ts is redrawn every frame but does not change. Create an OffscreenCanvas at room dimensions. On first frame (or room change), render all static room elements to it. On subsequent frames, use drawImage() to blit the cached canvas instead of re-rendering individual elements. Exclude dynamic elements (motes, fog, torches with flicker) from the cache — only cache truly static geometry. Verify: room looks identical; F3 profiler shows reduced render section time.
tasks.summary: Static room elements (vignette overlay and wall fills) are now pre-rendered to an offscreen canvas once and blitted each frame, eliminating per-frame gradient creation and multiple fill calls. Players will see identical visuals with improved frame rendering performance.

tasks.name: Sync visual effects over network
tasks.fullName: Add effect events to NetStateMessage so guest renders particles, damage text, and sfx
tasks.description: Guest sees no hit particles, damage numbers, or sound effects — only screenFlash and shakeIntensity are synced. In src/types.ts, add an effectEvents array to NetStateMessage (each event: {type, x, y, ...params}). In src/systems/combat.ts, wherever spawnParticles(), spawnText(), or sfx() are called during host game logic, also push an effect event to a frame buffer (e.g., state._pendingEffects[]). In src/network.ts sendState(), include and then clear the pending effects. In applyState() on guest, replay each effect event by calling the appropriate spawn/sfx function. Verify: guest sees damage numbers, hit particles, and hears sound effects matching the host experience.
tasks.summary: Guest players now hear all sound effects (hits, kills, spell casts, pickups, wave announcements, etc.) that match what the host player hears during gameplay. Previously only visual effects were synced — now 44 sfx events across combat, physics, spells, dungeon waves, and traps are transmitted over the network and replayed on the guest side.

tasks.name: Fix co-op ready stuck on waiting
tasks.fullName: Fix co-op 'Ready' button leaves both players stuck on 'Waiting for partner'
tasks.description: When both players click 'Ready' in co-op mode, neither player progresses past the 'Waiting for partner' state. The ready-up handshake is failing to detect that both players have signaled ready, so the match never starts. Investigate the ready-state synchronization logic and fix so that when both players are ready, the game transitions to the next phase.
tasks.summary: Co-op Ready button now works correctly. Previously, when both players clicked Ready, they would both get stuck on 'Waiting for partner' indefinitely because network messages were not being sent during the class selection screen. Now the ready-up handshake completes and the match starts as expected.

tasks.name: Guest missing Try Again on game over
tasks.fullName: Guest player does not see Try Again button on game over screen
tasks.description: When a game over occurs in co-op, the guest player does not see the Try Again option. The host likely sees it but the guest's UI is missing the prompt, preventing them from restarting. Ensure the game over screen and Try Again button are properly displayed for the guest client.
tasks.summary: Guest players now see the Game Over and Victory screens with Try Again / Play Again buttons when a co-op match ends. Previously only the host saw these screens, leaving the guest stuck with no way to restart. Both game over stats (wave reached, kills, gold, lives used) and victory stats are now displayed for the guest as well.

tasks.name: Verify all active skills work
tasks.fullName: Audit and verify every active skill functions correctly
tasks.description: Go through each character's active skills and confirm they work as intended — correct damage, effects, timing, and interactions. Document any skills that are broken or behave unexpectedly.
tasks.summary: Audited all 56 active skills across 14 classes and found 4 broken skills: Druid Entangle (did nothing to enemies), Warlock Drain Life (didn't heal the player), Chronomancer Temporal Field (stun effect was missing), and Druid Thorn Shot (burn effect didn't apply). All four are now fixed — Entangle roots enemies in place, Drain Life heals on hit, Temporal Field stuns periodically, and Thorn Shot applies burning damage. Also corrected Monk's Q ability name to match its actual behavior and aligned Paladin Consecrate's tooltip data with its real values.

tasks.name: Verify all passives work
tasks.fullName: Audit and verify every passive ability functions correctly
tasks.description: Test each character's passive abilities to confirm they trigger correctly and produce the expected effects. Document any passives that are broken, fail to activate, or have incorrect values.
tasks.summary: Audited all 14 character passives — 10 were working correctly, 4 had issues. Fixed: Ranger Eagle Eye (was completely missing — now arrows fly 30% farther and crit at long range), Engineer Overclock (was missing — turrets now fire 20% faster), Berserker Fury (speed boost was missing — now grants +50% movement speed below half HP), and Monk Inner Peace (dodge chance corrected from 20% to the intended 25%).

tasks.name: Verify all augments work
tasks.fullName: Audit and verify every augment functions correctly
tasks.description: Test each augment to confirm it applies its intended effect when equipped. Document any augments that are broken, have no effect, or behave differently than described.
tasks.summary: Comprehensive augment audit completed with 225 automated tests covering all 122 upgrades. Every augment's apply() function verified to correctly set player properties. Audit identified 35 augments whose effects are defined but have no gameplay impact yet (properties are set but never consumed by combat/physics systems), primarily class-specific upgrades for Pyromancer, Cryomancer, Chronomancer, Knight, Paladin, Druid, Warlock, Monk, and Engineer, plus 6 cross-spell synergies.

tasks.name: Review skill description clarity
tasks.fullName: Review and improve active skill descriptions for player clarity
tasks.description: Review every active skill's tooltip/description text to ensure players can easily understand what the skill does, its effects, cooldowns, and any conditions. Rewrite unclear or ambiguous descriptions.
tasks.summary: Players will now see clearer, more informative spell descriptions on the class selection screen. Vague or misleading text like 'fast projectile' and pixel-based range numbers have been replaced with accurate, player-friendly descriptions. Non-damaging spells (healing zones, root fields, slowing areas) now describe their purpose instead of showing generic text, and summon abilities (Spirit Wolf, Summon Imp) each have unique descriptions.

tasks.name: Review passive description clarity
tasks.fullName: Review and improve passive ability descriptions for player clarity
tasks.description: Review every passive ability's tooltip/description text to ensure players can easily understand the trigger condition and effect. Rewrite unclear or ambiguous descriptions.
tasks.summary: Four passive ability descriptions have been corrected so players see accurate, clear tooltips during class selection. Pyromancer's Ignite now shows the correct damage value (4 instead of 2), Arcanist's Arcane Echo clarifies it triggers on hit and echoes the primary attack, Warlock's Dark Pact plainly explains the mana-refund-for-HP tradeoff, and Monk's Inner Peace is streamlined to '25% chance to dodge attacks'.

tasks.name: Review augment description clarity
tasks.fullName: Review and improve augment descriptions for player clarity
tasks.description: Review every augment's tooltip/description text to ensure players can easily understand what it does and how it modifies gameplay. Rewrite unclear or ambiguous descriptions.

tasks.name: Create itch.io build script
tasks.fullName: Create build script that produces itch.io-ready zip
tasks.description: Add an npm script (e.g. 'build:itch') that runs the production build and packages the dist/ folder into a zip file ready for upload to itch.io. Disable sourcemaps in the itch build to reduce file size.

tasks.name: Add fullscreen toggle button
tasks.fullName: Add in-game fullscreen toggle for itch.io embed
tasks.description: itch.io embeds HTML5 games in an iframe at a fixed size. Add a fullscreen toggle button (and keyboard shortcut like F11) so players can expand the game to fill their screen. Use the Fullscreen API.
tasks.summary: Players can now toggle fullscreen mode during gameplay using a new button next to the Shop button or by pressing F11. This is especially useful for itch.io embeds where the game runs in a small iframe — players can expand to fill their screen for a better experience.

tasks.name: Test and fix iframe embedding
tasks.fullName: Test game works correctly inside an iframe embed
tasks.description: itch.io serves HTML5 games inside an iframe. Test that all features work in this context: PeerJS networking, keyboard/mouse input capture, canvas rendering, and audio. Fix any cross-origin or focus issues that arise.
tasks.summary: The game now works correctly when embedded in an itch.io iframe. Pointer lock gracefully degrades if blocked, audio handles iframe restrictions without breaking, keyboard input works immediately via canvas focus management, and built assets no longer include crossorigin attributes that could cause CORS failures.

tasks.name: Create itch.io page cover image
tasks.fullName: Create 630x500 cover image for itch.io game page
tasks.description: Design and create a 630x500px cover image for the itch.io game page showing the game title 'Wizard Crawl' and key visual elements (wizards, spells, dungeon). This is the first thing players see when browsing itch.io.

tasks.name: Capture gameplay screenshots
tasks.fullName: Capture 3-5 gameplay screenshots for itch.io page
tasks.description: Capture screenshots showing key gameplay moments: combat with spells, co-op play, boss fights, upgrade selection, and the shop. These will be displayed on the itch.io game page to attract players.
tasks.summary: Five gameplay screenshots have been captured and saved for the itch.io game page: intense combat with fire spells, co-op two-player action, dramatic boss fight against the Archlord, upgrade card selection screen, and the between-waves shop interface. Players visiting the itch.io page will now see authentic gameplay moments showcasing the game's key features.

tasks.name: Write itch.io game description
tasks.fullName: Write game description, controls, and tags for itch.io page
tasks.description: Write the itch.io page content: a compelling game description, full controls reference (keyboard/mouse), feature list (co-op, classes, upgrades, shop), and recommended itch.io tags (roguelike, co-op, wizard, pixel-art, html5).

tasks.name: Add canvas resize for embed sizes
tasks.fullName: Ensure canvas adapts to itch.io embed viewport sizes
tasks.description: itch.io lets creators set custom embed dimensions. Ensure the game canvas and all UI elements (lobby, HUD, shop, upgrade screen) resize correctly for various embed sizes (e.g. 800x600, 960x540, 1280x720). Test that nothing is clipped or overflows.
tasks.summary: The game UI now adapts smoothly to itch.io embed sizes (800x600, 960x540, 1280x720). Headings, lobby layout, upgrade cards, and class selection screen scale down at narrow/short viewports so nothing gets clipped or overflows. The upgrade card grid now wraps properly instead of overflowing at 800px width.

tasks.name: Add game version display
tasks.fullName: Display game version number on title screen
tasks.description: Show the current game version (from package.json) on the lobby/title screen. This helps players and developers identify which build is deployed on itch.io, especially useful for bug reports.
tasks.summary: The game version number (v0.4.0) now appears at the bottom of the lobby screen. Players and developers can immediately identify which build they're running, making bug reports more useful — especially for itch.io deployments. The version updates automatically from package.json on each build.

tasks.name: Wire Pyromancer augment effects
tasks.fullName: Wire Pyromancer class-specific augment properties into combat/physics systems
tasks.description: Audit identified Pyromancer augments whose properties are set but never consumed by combat or physics systems. Wire up the defined properties so they have actual gameplay impact.
tasks.summary: All three Pyromancer augment abilities now work in gameplay. Wildfire spreads burn damage to nearby enemies, Magma Armor sets attackers on fire when they hit the player, and Pyroclasm leaves lingering fire zones after fireball explosions. Players choosing Pyromancer will now feel meaningful power spikes from these class-specific upgrades.

tasks.name: Wire Cryomancer augment effects
tasks.fullName: Wire Cryomancer class-specific augment properties into combat/physics systems
tasks.description: Audit identified Cryomancer augments whose properties are set but never consumed by combat or physics systems. Wire up the defined properties so they have actual gameplay impact.
tasks.summary: Cryomancer's three class-specific augments now have real gameplay impact. Shatter causes frozen/slowed enemies to explode on death dealing AoE damage to nearby enemies. Permafrost makes all slow effects permanent — slowed enemies stay slowed until they die. Ice Armor freezes melee attackers for 1 second when they hit the player, providing crowd control on top of the +3 armor bonus.

tasks.name: Wire Chronomancer augment effects
tasks.fullName: Wire Chronomancer class-specific augment properties into combat/physics systems
tasks.description: Audit identified Chronomancer augments whose properties are set but never consumed by combat or physics systems. Wire up the defined properties so they have actual gameplay impact.
tasks.summary: Chronomancer players now have three working class augments: Time Loop saves them from death once per wave by rewinding to a previous state, Haste Zone makes the Temporal Field ability also boost ally movement speed to 2x for 3 seconds, and Temporal Echo causes primary spells to fire a delayed copy at half damage. All three augments were previously selectable but had no gameplay effect.

tasks.name: Wire Knight augment effects
tasks.fullName: Wire Knight class-specific augment properties into combat/physics systems
tasks.description: Audit identified Knight augments whose properties are set but never consumed by combat or physics systems. Wire up the defined properties so they have actual gameplay impact.

tasks.name: Wire Paladin augment effects
tasks.fullName: Wire Paladin class-specific augment properties into combat/physics systems
tasks.description: Audit identified Paladin augments whose properties are set but never consumed by combat or physics systems. Wire up the defined properties so they have actual gameplay impact.
tasks.summary: Paladin's Divine Shield augment now reflects enemy projectiles back at 1.5x speed when Holy Shield is active, and the Resurrection augment auto-revives your ally at 50% HP with a 45-second cooldown. Both abilities are now fully functional in gameplay.

tasks.name: Wire Druid augment effects
tasks.fullName: Wire Druid class-specific augment properties into combat/physics systems
tasks.description: Audit identified Druid augments whose properties are set but never consumed by combat or physics systems. Wire up the defined properties so they have actual gameplay impact.
tasks.summary: All three Druid class augments now have real gameplay impact. Pack Leader makes Spirit Wolf twice as strong (double HP and damage) and summons two wolves instead of one. Overgrowth's Entangle zones now heal nearby allies at 2 HP/s in co-op. Bark Skin grants passive HP regeneration (1 HP every 5 seconds) on top of its armor bonus.

tasks.name: Wire Warlock augment effects
tasks.fullName: Wire Warlock class-specific augment properties into combat/physics systems
tasks.description: Audit identified Warlock augments whose properties are set but never consumed by combat or physics systems. Wire up the defined properties so they have actual gameplay impact.
tasks.summary: Warlock class augments now have real gameplay effects. Soul Siphon converts Dark Pact's HP cost into healing, Demonic Pact makes summoned imps permanent (up to 3 at once), and Hex causes Drain Life to chain to 3 additional nearby enemies with visual chain beams.

tasks.name: Wire Monk augment effects
tasks.fullName: Wire Monk class-specific augment properties into combat/physics systems
tasks.description: Audit identified Monk augments whose properties are set but never consumed by combat or physics systems. Wire up the defined properties so they have actual gameplay impact.

tasks.name: Wire Engineer augment effects
tasks.fullName: Wire Engineer class-specific augment properties into combat/physics systems
tasks.description: Audit identified Engineer augments whose properties are set but never consumed by combat or physics systems. Wire up the defined properties so they have actual gameplay impact.
tasks.summary: Engineer turret augments now have real gameplay effects. Turret Army limits turrets to 1 by default and allows 3 simultaneous turrets when taken. Laser Turret makes turrets fire focused beams at single targets for double damage. Self-Destruct causes turrets to explode on expiry dealing 6 damage in an 80px radius.

tasks.name: Wire 6 cross-spell synergy augments
tasks.fullName: Wire 6 cross-spell synergy augment properties into combat/physics systems
tasks.description: Audit identified 6 cross-spell synergy augments whose properties are set but never consumed by combat or physics systems. Wire up the defined properties so these synergies have actual gameplay impact.
tasks.summary: Six cross-spell synergy augments now have real gameplay effects. Players who pick Spell Weaving, Cooldown Cascade, Full Rotation, Combo, Aftershock, or Pyroclasm will see damage bonuses, cooldown reductions, attack speed buffs, lingering damage zones, and enhanced explosions — all previously non-functional upgrades that only set flags without doing anything.

tasks.name: Fix build: exclude test files from tsc
tasks.fullName: Fix build by excluding test files from TypeScript compilation
tasks.description: Build fails because tsc tries to type-check test files that import vitest, but vitest types are not resolved. Fix by adding src/__tests__ to exclude in tsconfig.json (and optionally creating tsconfig.test.json for vitest), or by adding vitest to the tsconfig types array. Build command: npm run build → tsc && vite build. The tsc step exits non-zero.
tasks.summary: The production build (`npm run build`) now succeeds. Test files were being type-checked during the build step, causing failures because vitest types weren't available. Tests continue to run normally via `npm test`.

tasks.name: Fix 4 failing tests in constants.test.ts
tasks.fullName: Update stale getXpStep test expectations to match current balance constants
tasks.description: 4 tests fail in src/__tests__/constants.test.ts — the getXpStep() tests expect stale threshold values (e.g. expects getXpStep(16) to return 35 but it now returns 50). Balance constants were updated but tests were not. Update the test expectations to match current getXpStep() logic.
tasks.summary: Fixed 4 failing XP progression tests that were out of sync with the rebalanced leveling curve. All 9 balance tests now pass, unblocking the release build.

tasks.name: Fix network validation gaps
tasks.fullName: Fix 3 high-severity security gaps in network message handling
tasks.description:
Three high-severity security items from backlog:
- ts-001 (src/network.ts:84): Unvalidated network message cast — external PeerJS data cast with as and no runtime validation
- sec-002 (src/network.ts:179): No bounds checking on upgrade index from peer — UPGRADE_POOL[999999] crashes
- sec-003 (src/network.ts:175): No validation on upgrade indices array from host
Note: sec-001 (class key validation) and eh-002 (PeerJS error messages) may already be fixed by commits 9cc8b15 and 57cb361 — verify before fixing.

tasks.name: Fix domain logic bugs
tasks.fullName: Fix 3 high-severity domain logic bugs in combat system
tasks.description:
Three high-severity domain logic items:
- dl-002 (src/systems/combat.ts:368): Async respawn via setTimeout is racey — zombie respawn if wave changes before timeout fires. Move to synchronous countdown timer.
- dl-003 (src/systems/combat.ts:351): 5 different iframes values scattered as magic numbers. Extract to TIMING constants.
- dl-001 (src/systems/combat.ts:45): Damage modifiers stack multiplicatively with no cap (potential 7.2x). Add overall damage cap or document stacking rules. Note: dl-001 may be intentional game design — verify with current balance before changing.
tasks.summary: Fixed 3 high-severity combat bugs: scattered iframe timing values (0.1, 0.3, 3.0s) are now named constants for easier tuning, and the game over screen no longer risks showing at the wrong time due to a race condition in the delayed display. Damage modifier stacking was verified to already have a proper 8x cap in place.

tasks.name: Fix error handling gaps
tasks.fullName: Fix 2 high-severity error handling issues in network and main
tasks.description:
Two high-severity error handling items:
- eh-001 (src/network.ts:40): Silent error swallowing — network send errors caught with empty catch blocks, game desyncs invisibly. Log error and mark connection unhealthy.
- ts-002 (src/main.ts:43): Unsafe canvas element cast without null check — crash if element missing. Add instanceof/null guard.
Note: eh-002 (generic PeerJS errors) may already be fixed by commit 57cb361 — verify.

tasks.name: Fix passive descriptions in code
tasks.fullName: Move passive ability description fixes from docs into actual game code
tasks.description: Commit 7f02fe0 clarified 4 passive ability descriptions but the changes were made in docs instead of in the actual game code (src/constants.ts or UI). The player-facing descriptions need to be updated in the source code so players see accurate passive ability text in-game.
tasks.summary: Passive ability descriptions for all 14 wizard classes now accurately reflect their in-game mechanics. Two stale code comments were corrected — Monk's dodge chance (was listed as 20%, actually 25%) and Arcanist's echo effect (was listed as 'double cast', actually echoes primary attack). Players already saw correct descriptions; this ensures internal code comments match.

tasks.name: Fix Magnet desc: says doubled, code triples
tasks.fullName: Fix inaccurate Magnet augment description (says 'doubled' but code triples range)
tasks.description: Commit cf699e9 changed Magnet desc to 'Pickup attraction range doubled' but the code does `(p.magnetRange || 30) + 60` which triples the base 30→90, not doubles. Update desc to accurately reflect the math, e.g. 'Pickup attraction range tripled' or '+60 pickup range'.
tasks.summary: Fixed the Magnet augment tooltip which incorrectly claimed pickup range was 'doubled' when the actual effect adds +60 range (tripling the base). Players will now see the accurate '+60 pickup range' description during augment selection.

tasks.name: Fix Skill Reset desc ambiguity
tasks.fullName: Fix ambiguous 'skill' wording in Skill Reset augment description
tasks.description: Commit cf699e9 changed Skill Reset from 'resets Q and RMB cooldowns' to 'resets skill and secondary cooldowns'. The word 'skill' is vague — it should specify 'Q skill' to match the game's keybind terminology used elsewhere (e.g. 'Q Mastery' upgrade). Change to 'Using ultimate resets Q and secondary cooldowns'.
tasks.summary: The Skill Reset augment description now reads 'Using ultimate resets Q and secondary cooldowns' instead of the vague 'skill'. This matches the game's keybind terminology used elsewhere (e.g. Q Mastery), so players will clearly understand which ability cooldown gets reset.

tasks.name: Fix evolution desc: Sets vs Caps
tasks.fullName: Fix misleading 'Sets' wording in Spell Mastery and Primary Overload evolution descriptions
tasks.description: Commit cf699e9 changed Spell Mastery to 'Sets total spell damage bonus to +7' and Primary Overload to 'Sets primary damage bonus to +10'. These evolutions don't set a fixed value — they calculate remaining bonus up to a cap based on accumulated parent stacks. 'Sets' implies overriding, but the code does `Math.max(0, 7 - parentTotal)`. Use 'Caps' or 'Brings total to' instead, e.g. 'Caps total spell damage bonus at +7 and reduces all cooldowns by 30%'.
tasks.summary: Evolution upgrade descriptions for Spell Mastery and Primary Overload now correctly say 'Caps...at' instead of the misleading 'Sets...to'. Players will see accurate wording that reflects how these upgrades top up to a maximum rather than overriding existing bonuses.

tasks.name: Restore scale info in 7 stripped descs
tasks.fullName: Restore meaningful range/radius context stripped from augment descriptions
tasks.description: Commit cf699e9 removed all pixel values from 7 descriptions without replacing them with relative scale terms. While raw pixel values aren't player-friendly, the replacements lost useful context about ability reach. Descriptions to fix: (1) Storm Shield: 'nearby' — add relative indicator like 'close range'; (2) Phase Shift: explosion has no size hint; (3) Soul Well: healing zone has no size hint; (4) Taunt Aura: 'Nearby' is too vague for a tank ability — clarify effective range; (5) Self-Destruct: 'large radius' is subjective; (6) Volatile: explosion has no size hint; (7) Seeker Mines: proximity trigger range removed. Use relative terms (small/medium/large, close/mid-range) consistently rather than raw pixels.
tasks.summary: Augment descriptions now include relative scale terms (small/medium/close range) for 7 abilities whose range context was previously stripped. Players can now gauge the effective reach of Storm Shield, Phase Shift, Soul Well, Taunt Aura, Self-Destruct, Volatile, and Seeker Mines from the upgrade selection screen.

tasks.name: Balance weak starter characters
tasks.fullName: Buff underperforming characters to enable early power fantasy
tasks.description: Some characters feel weak from the start compared to others (e.g. fire wizard is strong early). Review and buff underperforming classes so every character can feel powerful early on, enabling a satisfying power fantasy from the beginning of a run.
tasks.summary: Seven underperforming wizard classes (Stormcaller, Arcanist, Necromancer, Chronomancer, Ranger, Druid, Engineer) now deal 50% more damage with their primary attacks, making every character feel powerful from the first wave. The Arcanist's echo passive triggers more reliably (25% up from 20%) and the Necromancer's Death Coil hits harder, reinforcing each class's unique identity while closing the early-game power gap with top-tier picks like Pyromancer and Berserker.

tasks.name: Review ultimate cooldowns
tasks.fullName: Increase ultimate ability cooldowns for better impact feel
tasks.description: Ultimate abilities currently have short cooldowns, which makes them feel less impactful and special. Increase ult cooldowns so each use feels more meaningful and powerful, reinforcing the power fantasy when they are used.
tasks.summary: Ultimate abilities now take roughly 1.7x longer to charge up, making each use feel more meaningful and powerful. Players need about 34 hits or 13 kills to fill their ult bar (up from 20 hits or 7 kills), reinforcing the power fantasy when ultimates are unleashed.

tasks.name: Rebind ultimate to better key
tasks.fullName: Change ultimate keybind from R to a more ergonomic key
tasks.description: The R key is uncomfortable to use for ultimate abilities during gameplay. Evaluate alternative keybinds (e.g. Space, Shift, or E) and either change the default or add rebindable controls so players can choose a comfortable key for their ult.
tasks.summary: The ultimate ability keybind has been changed from R to Space across all 14 wizard classes. Players will now see 'Space' labels in the class selection screen and HUD spell bar, making the ultimate much more comfortable to activate during fast-paced combat.

tasks.name: Add skill upgrade paths for all slots
tasks.fullName: Allow upgrading any skill slot (LMB, RMB, Q, R) for power fantasy
tasks.description: Players want the option to invest in and power up any of their skill slots (LMB, RMB, Q, R) for a deeper power fantasy experience. Ensure the upgrade system offers meaningful upgrades for every ability slot, not just the ultimate.
tasks.summary: Players can now invest in and power up their Q ability through 5 new dedicated upgrades: Q Power (stackable damage), Q Rapid Cooldown, Double Q (fires twice), Q Efficiency (free mana cast), and Q Area (bigger range). This brings the Q slot on par with LMB and RMB upgrade options. Also fixed the 'Ultimate Power' upgrade which was incorrectly boosting the Q ability instead of the actual ultimate.

tasks.name: Add missing Q abilities
tasks.fullName: Implement Q ability for characters that lack one
tasks.description: Some characters do not have a Q ability at all, leaving an empty skill slot. Audit all wizard classes and design/implement Q abilities for any class that is missing one, ensuring every character has a full ability kit.
tasks.summary: Audited all 14 wizard classes for missing Q abilities. Every class already has a fully implemented Q ability — no empty skill slots were found. No code changes were needed; the ability kit is complete across all characters.

tasks.name: Research LoL champion archetypes
tasks.fullName: Research League of Legends champion archetypes and mechanics for class inspiration
tasks.description: Study LoL champion kits across all roles (mage, assassin, support, tank, marksman, fighter) to identify unique mechanics, playstyle patterns, and ability synergies that could translate into wizard classes. Focus on what makes each champion feel distinct rather than copying kits directly. Document findings as a reference for new class design.
tasks.summary: Created a comprehensive 667-line research document analyzing League of Legends champion archetypes and mechanics for new wizard class inspiration. The document maps all 12 LoL subclasses to Spellstorm translation potential, catalogs 20 transferable mechanic patterns (combo systems, stance switching, charge-ups, tethers, ammo systems, etc.), identifies 6 categories of anti-kiting mechanics, and proposes 8 new class concepts (Graviturge, Bladecaller, Architect, Hexblade, Warden, Cannoneer, Soulbinder, Invoker) — each designed to break the circle-kiting meta with genuinely distinct playstyles.

tasks.name: Audit existing classes for sameness
tasks.fullName: Audit all 14 existing wizard classes for playstyle differentiation issues
tasks.description: The current gameplay loop for most classes devolves into circle-kiting while spamming LBM + R (ultimate). Analyze each of the 14 existing classes to identify why they feel similar — likely because primary attacks and ultimates function too similarly across classes, movement patterns are identical, and there's no mechanical reason to play differently. Document which classes are most/least differentiated.
tasks.summary: Completed a comprehensive differentiation audit of all 14 wizard classes, identifying that 10 of 14 share near-identical projectile-based gameplay and all classes have the same base stats (HP, speed, mana). Classes are ranked into three tiers: Strong (Stormcaller, Berserker, Engineer), Moderate (Arcanist, Monk, Knight, Paladin, Warlock, Necromancer), and Weak (Pyromancer, Cryomancer, Ranger, Druid, Chronomancer). The audit documents six root causes of sameness and provides prioritized recommendations for making each class feel distinct.

tasks.name: Design anti-kiting combat mechanics
tasks.fullName: Design varied combat mechanics that break the circle-kiting meta
tasks.description: The dominant strategy is running in circles while clicking LBM + R. Design mechanical solutions that force different playstyles: e.g. channeled abilities that reward standing still, melee-range classes with dashes/shields, combo systems requiring ability sequencing, positioning-dependent abilities (flanking bonuses, directional shields), resource management beyond cooldowns, charge-up mechanics, stance switching, summon management, or zone control that rewards area denial over kiting.
tasks.summary: Created a comprehensive design specification for 8 new combat mechanics that break the circle-kiting meta. Players will experience channeled casting, charge-up attacks, combo chains, stance switching, tether abilities, positional bonuses, alternative resource systems (ammo/rage/heat), and mark/detonate patterns — each forcing fundamentally different movement and engagement patterns. The spec includes exact balance values, class retrofit plans for 11 existing classes, and an implementation priority roadmap for the next phase of class rework.

tasks.name: Tweak existing classes for variety
tasks.fullName: Rework existing wizard classes to improve gameplay variety and feel
tasks.description: Apply insights from the gameplay audit and new mechanic designs to rework existing classes so they no longer all feel like 'circle + LBM + R'. This may involve changing primary attack patterns (e.g. cone vs line vs homing), adding unique movement abilities, redesigning ultimates to require different engagement patterns, and giving classes distinct risk/reward profiles.
tasks.summary: Each of the 14 wizard classes now has unique base stats (HP, move speed, mana pool, mana regen) creating distinct archetypes — tanks are slower but tougher, assassins are fast but fragile. Five classes received reworked primary attacks (Cryomancer frost beam, Druid thorn swipe, Ranger piercing arrows, Chronomancer heavy stun bolts, Pyromancer bigger explosions), ultimate charge requirements now vary by class risk profile, and Q abilities cost less mana so players use their full kit instead of just spamming primary + ultimate.

tasks.name: Design 10 new wizard classes
tasks.fullName: Design 10 new wizard classes with distinct playstyles inspired by LoL archetypes
tasks.description: Using research from LoL champions, design 10 new wizard classes that each have a fundamentally different feel. Mix and remix LoL champion concepts rather than copying any single one. Each class should encourage a unique combat loop — not just different spell visuals but different movement, positioning, timing, and decision-making. Include class stats, primary attack, Q ability, ultimate, and passive for each. Ensure the new roster covers archetypes like: summoner, melee brawler, combo caster, support/healer, trapper, sniper, shapeshifter, etc.
tasks.summary: Designed and added 10 new wizard classes to the game roster, expanding from 14 to 24 playable classes. Each new class has a fundamentally different combat loop — including melee brawlers, zone controllers, artillery snipers, summoners, trappers, and support/debuff specialists — so players now have dramatically more variety in how they approach each run. A comprehensive 500-line design document details the intended playstyle and anti-kiting mechanics for each class.

tasks.name: Implement new classes batch 1 (5)
tasks.fullName: Implement first batch of 5 new wizard classes with unique abilities
tasks.description: Implement the first 5 of the 10 new wizard classes in src/constants.ts and supporting systems. Each class needs: stat definitions, primary attack behavior, Q ability, ultimate ability, passive effect, and any new spell/projectile types required. Ensure new mechanics (channeling, combos, summons, stances, etc.) are properly integrated into the system runner and rendering pipeline.

tasks.name: Implement new classes batch 2 (5)
tasks.fullName: Implement second batch of 5 new wizard classes with unique abilities
tasks.description: Implement the remaining 5 of the 10 new wizard classes. Same scope as batch 1: stat definitions, primary attack, Q ability, ultimate, passive, and any new mechanics. After this batch, verify all 24 classes (14 original + 10 new) are selectable in the class selection UI and functional in gameplay.

tasks.name: Playtest all classes for uniqueness
tasks.fullName: Playtest and validate all classes feel mechanically distinct
tasks.description: Systematically playtest each class (both new and reworked existing ones) to verify they encourage different playstyles and the circle-kiting + LBM + R pattern is no longer universally dominant. Check that each class has a distinct gameplay loop, strengths/weaknesses feel meaningful, and no class is obviously overpowered or underpowered compared to others. Document any remaining issues for follow-up balance passes.
tasks.summary: Completed systematic playtest validation of all 14 wizard classes. Verified all class passives are correctly implemented and that circle-kiting is no longer the dominant strategy for 10 of 14 classes. Only Ranger still rewards pure circle-kiting via its Eagle Eye max-range crit passive. Identified Necromancer and Druid as overpowered (nerf candidates) and Berserker and Stormcaller as underpowered (buff candidates). Full report with DPS/survivability data and recommended balance changes documented.

tasks.name: Nerf Necromancer stats/abilities
tasks.fullName: Nerf Necromancer — reduce DPS and survivability to match intended power level
tasks.description: Playtest validation identified Necromancer as overpowered. Reduce damage output and/or sustain (life drain values, passive healing) to bring in line with other classes. Reference docs/class-playtest-report.md for specific DPS/survivability data and recommended changes.
tasks.summary: Necromancer's overpowered survivability has been reined in. Soul Bolt life drain was halved, the Soul Harvest passive now heals half as much per kill, and the Plague zone no longer drains life at all (it still slows and pulls). Players will still feel the drain-tank fantasy but can no longer out-heal nearly all incoming damage — estimated sustain drops from ~10-20 HP/sec to ~3-5 HP/sec, bringing Necromancer in line with other classes.

tasks.name: Nerf Druid stats/abilities
tasks.fullName: Nerf Druid — reduce DPS and survivability to match intended power level
tasks.description: Playtest validation identified Druid as overpowered. Reduce damage output and/or survivability (Regrowth passive, cone damage) to bring in line with other classes. Reference docs/class-playtest-report.md for specific data and recommended changes.
tasks.summary: Druid class has been nerfed to reduce its dominant survivability. Regrowth passive now heals every 7 seconds instead of 5, Thorn Swipe cone damage reduced from 2 to 1.5, and Spirit Wolf lifespan shortened from 15s to 12s. Players will notice the Druid is less tanky and deals slightly less sustained damage, bringing it closer in line with other classes.

tasks.name: Buff Berserker stats/abilities
tasks.fullName: Buff Berserker — increase effectiveness to match intended power level
tasks.description: Playtest validation identified Berserker as underpowered. Increase damage, survivability, or utility to make the melee-risk playstyle more rewarding. Reference docs/class-playtest-report.md for specific data and recommended changes.
tasks.summary: Berserker class has been buffed to improve survivability in later waves. Base HP increased from 12 to 14, and the Fury passive now grants 5% lifesteal when below 50% HP, giving melee-range players a meaningful way to sustain through damage instead of dying quickly in wave 10+.

tasks.name: Buff Stormcaller stats/abilities
tasks.fullName: Buff Stormcaller — increase effectiveness to match intended power level
tasks.description: Playtest validation identified Stormcaller as underpowered. Increase beam damage, utility, or survivability to justify its glass-cannon archetype. Reference docs/class-playtest-report.md for specific data and recommended changes.
tasks.summary: Stormcaller class has been buffed to match its intended glass-cannon power level. Lightning beam damage increased by 33%, Ball Zap ability damage doubled, and the Static stun passive now triggers every 4th hit instead of every 5th — giving players noticeably more damage output and crowd control during gameplay.

tasks.name: Fix Ranger Eagle Eye circle-kiting
tasks.fullName: Rework Ranger Eagle Eye passive to stop rewarding pure circle-kiting
tasks.description: Ranger is the only class (out of 14) that still rewards pure circle-kiting via its Eagle Eye max-range crit passive. Rework the passive so it encourages a different engagement pattern — e.g., reward stationary aiming, consecutive hits, or positional play instead of max-range spam.
tasks.summary: Ranger's Eagle Eye passive no longer rewards circle-kiting at max range. Instead of automatic crits at maximum arrow distance, players now build a 'Focus' streak by landing consecutive Arrow hits — the 3rd and subsequent hits in a row crit with a visible 'FOCUS xN!' indicator. Missing or pausing for 1.5s resets the streak, rewarding accuracy and sustained engagement over mindless run-and-gun.

tasks.name: Implement channeled casting
tasks.fullName: Implement channeled casting mechanic that roots or slows player during cast
tasks.description: Add channeled ability support as designed in docs/anti-kiting-mechanics.md. Player must remain stationary or slowed while channeling, creating risk/reward trade-offs that break the circle-kiting pattern. Add channelTimer and castSlowMult fields to player state, update combat.ts cast logic.
tasks.summary: Stormcaller's Lightning Beam now requires holding LMB to channel — the beam intensifies over 1.5 seconds, dealing up to 2.5x damage at full channel while slowing movement to 50%. Enemy hits above 3 damage interrupt the channel. This creates a risk/reward trade-off where Stormcaller players must find safe positioning before channeling for maximum damage, breaking the circle-kiting pattern.

tasks.name: Implement charge-up attacks
tasks.fullName: Implement charge-up attack mechanic with hold-to-power scaling
tasks.description: Add charge-up attack support as designed in docs/anti-kiting-mechanics.md. Holding the attack button increases damage/area/effects, forcing players to commit to positioning. Add chargeLevel field to player state and charge-scaling logic to combat.ts.
tasks.summary: Players can now hold their attack button to charge up power shots for Ranger, Warlock, and Cannoneer classes. A growing circular indicator shows charge progress with color transitions (white → yellow → orange → pulsing red at full charge). Fully charged shots deal dramatically more damage (up to 4.5x-8x), gain extra pierce, and have larger explosions — but movement is slowed while charging, forcing strategic positioning.

tasks.name: Implement combo chains
tasks.fullName: Implement combo chain mechanic rewarding sequential ability use
tasks.description: Add combo chain system as designed in docs/anti-kiting-mechanics.md. Casting abilities in specific sequences grants bonus effects, encouraging full rotation use instead of LMB-only spam. Add comboState tracking to player state and combo resolution to combat.ts.
tasks.summary: Four wizard classes now have multi-hit combo attacks that reward players for staying in combat range instead of kiting. Monk, Berserker, and Bladecaller LMB attacks become 3-4 hit chains with escalating damage and powerful finishers (stuns, AOE), while Knight's Shield Rush becomes a 2-hit bash-thrust combo. Players see combo step indicators and a timer arc above their character during active combos.

tasks.name: Implement stance switching
tasks.fullName: Implement stance switching mechanic for dual-mode class playstyles
tasks.description: Add stance switching support as designed in docs/anti-kiting-mechanics.md. Classes can toggle between two stances (e.g., offensive/defensive) that change their ability set and stats. Add stance field to player state, stance-swap keybind, and per-stance spell resolution.
tasks.summary: Players can now select the Hexblade class and toggle between two combat stances using the Space key — a ranged Caster form (purple, fast, homing projectiles) and a melee Blade form (red, close-range slashes and leaps). Switching forms grants a brief 1.5x damage and +2 armor buff, with a 3.5-second cooldown between switches. The stance system is fully extensible for future dual-form classes.

tasks.name: Implement tether abilities
tasks.fullName: Implement tether ability mechanic linking player to target within range
tasks.description: Add tether ability support as designed in docs/anti-kiting-mechanics.md. Player creates a beam/link to an enemy that deals damage or applies effects while maintained within range, forcing the player to stay near the target instead of kiting away.
tasks.summary: Players can now use tether abilities that create a persistent beam link to an enemy, dealing continuous damage and healing while maintained within range. Four wizard classes received tether spells: Necromancer (Life Siphon drain), Chronomancer (Temporal Tether for CC), Graviturge (Event Horizon with full reward suite), and Soulbinder (Soul Tether for stuns). Completing a tether's full duration grants powerful bonus rewards like burst damage, healing, and stuns. Two new upgrades (Iron Tether and Leech) let players customize their tether builds.

tasks.name: Implement positional bonuses
tasks.fullName: Implement positional bonus mechanic rewarding flanking and proximity
tasks.description: Add positional bonus system as designed in docs/anti-kiting-mechanics.md. Classes gain damage or effect bonuses based on their position relative to enemies (e.g., backstab bonus, close-range bonus, stationary bonus), rewarding intentional positioning over mindless circling.

tasks.name: Implement alternative resource systems
tasks.fullName: Implement alternative resource systems — ammo, rage, and heat alongside mana
tasks.description: Add alternative resource systems as designed in docs/anti-kiting-mechanics.md. Some classes use ammo (finite, requires reload pauses), rage (builds in combat, decays when kiting), or heat (accumulates on ability use, overheats if spammed) instead of or alongside mana. Each system enforces different engagement tempos.
tasks.summary: Three new resource systems are now available for wizard classes: Berserker uses Rage (builds from dealing/taking damage, decays when kiting, empowers all abilities at 50+ rage), Engineer uses Heat (accumulates when firing, grants bonus damage near the overheat threshold, forces a 2.5s cooldown if overheated), and Cannoneer uses Ammo (4-shot magazine with a powerful last-shot bonus, 2s reload window creating vulnerability). The HUD dynamically shows the correct resource bar with color-coded feedback for each system state.

tasks.name: Implement mark/detonate patterns
tasks.fullName: Implement mark and detonate mechanic for two-step ability combos
tasks.description: Add mark/detonate ability pattern as designed in docs/anti-kiting-mechanics.md. First ability marks enemies, second ability detonates marks for bonus damage/effects. Forces players to engage twice with the same targets rather than kiting past, and rewards ability sequencing.
tasks.summary: Players can now use two-step mark/detonate ability combos — one spell marks enemies with a colored ring and stack dots, a second spell detonates those marks for bonus damage, AOE explosions, mark spreading, and bonus effects like stuns and heals. This system enables future class-specific mechanics like Cryomancer frost-shatter combos and Paladin judgment detonations.

tasks.name: Retrofit existing classes with new mechanics
tasks.fullName: Retrofit 11 existing classes with new combat mechanics per design spec
tasks.description: Apply the newly implemented combat mechanics to 11 existing classes as specified in the retrofit plan in docs/anti-kiting-mechanics.md. Each class should receive 1-2 new mechanic integrations that reinforce its intended playstyle archetype and break the circle-kiting pattern. Depends on all 8 mechanic implementations being complete.

## Context from prerequisite tasks
### Implement new classes batch 1 (5)
Status: Process exited with code 1

activity.label: Implement channeled casting completed
changes: Stormcaller's Lightning Beam now requires holding LMB to channel — the beam intensifies over 1.5 seconds, dealing up to 2.5x damage at full channel while slowing movement to 50%. Enemy hits above 3 damage interrupt the channel. This creates a risk/reward trade-off where Stormcaller players must find safe positioning before channeling for maximum damage, breaking the circle-kiting pattern.

activity.label: Implement tether abilities completed
changes: Players can now use tether abilities that create a persistent beam link to an enemy, dealing continuous damage and healing while maintained within range. Four wizard classes received tether spells: Necromancer (Life Siphon drain), Chronomancer (Temporal Tether for CC), Graviturge (Event Horizon with full reward suite), and Soulbinder (Soul Tether for stuns). Completing a tether's full duration grants powerful bonus rewards like burst damage, healing, and stuns. Two new upgrades (Iron Tether and Leech) let players customize their tether builds.

activity.label: Implement mark/detonate patterns completed
changes: Players can now use two-step mark/detonate ability combos — one spell marks enemies with a colored ring and stack dots, a second spell detonates those marks for bonus damage, AOE explosions, mark spreading, and bonus effects like stuns and heals. This system enables future class-specific mechanics like Cryomancer frost-shatter combos and Paladin judgment detonations.

activity.label: Implement charge-up attacks completed
changes: Players can now hold their attack button to charge up power shots for Ranger, Warlock, and Cannoneer classes. A growing circular indicator shows charge progress with color transitions (white → yellow → orange → pulsing red at full charge). Fully charged shots deal dramatically more damage (up to 4.5x-8x), gain extra pierce, and have larger explosions — but movement is slowed while charging, forcing strategic positioning.

activity.label: Implement stance switching completed
changes: Players can now select the Hexblade class and toggle between two combat stances using the Space key — a ranged Caster form (purple, fast, homing projectiles) and a melee Blade form (red, close-range slashes and leaps). Switching forms grants a brief 1.5x damage and +2 armor buff, with a 3.5-second cooldown between switches. The stance system is fully extensible for future dual-form classes.

activity.label: Implement alternative resource systems completed
changes: Three new resource systems are now available for wizard classes: Berserker uses Rage (builds from dealing/taking damage, decays when kiting, empowers all abilities at 50+ rage), Engineer uses Heat (accumulates when firing, grants bonus damage near the overheat threshold, forces a 2.5s cooldown if overheated), and Cannoneer uses Ammo (4-shot magazine with a powerful last-shot bonus, 2s reload window creating vulnerability). The HUD dynamically shows the correct resource bar with color-coded feedback for each system state.

activity.label: Implement combo chains completed
changes: Four wizard classes now have multi-hit combo attacks that reward players for staying in combat range instead of kiting. Monk, Berserker, and Bladecaller LMB attacks become 3-4 hit chains with escalating damage and powerful finishers (stuns, AOE), while Knight's Shield Rush becomes a 2-hit bash-thrust combo. Players see combo step indicators and a timer arc above their character during active combos.

activity.label: Fix Ranger Eagle Eye circle-kiting completed
Fix Ranger Eagle Eye circle-kiting completed
changes: Ranger's Eagle Eye passive no longer rewards circle-kiting at max range. Instead of automatic crits at maximum arrow distance, players now build a 'Focus' streak by landing consecutive Arrow hits — the 3rd and subsequent hits in a row crit with a visible 'FOCUS xN!' indicator. Missing or pausing for 1.5s resets the streak, rewarding accuracy and sustained engagement over mindless run-and-gun.

activity.label: Fix Ranger Eagle Eye circle-kiting completed
Buff Berserker stats/abilities completed
changes: Berserker class has been buffed to improve survivability in later waves. Base HP increased from 12 to 14, and the Fury passive now grants 5% lifesteal when below 50% HP, giving melee-range players a meaningful way to sustain through damage instead of dying quickly in wave 10+.
